252:

254:   end

1: module Enterprise::Inbox
2:   def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
3:     return super unless enable_auto_assignment?
4:     return filter_by_capacity(available_agents).map(&:user_id) if auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
5: 
6:     max_assignment_limit = auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit']
7:     overloaded_agent_ids = max_assignment_limit.present? ? get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(max_assignment_limit) : []
8:     super - overloaded_agent_ids
9:   end
10: 
11:   def active_bot?
12:     super || captain_active?
13:   end
14: 
15:   def captain_active?
16:     captain_assistant.present? && more_responses?
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def more_responses?
22:     account.usage_limits[:captain][:responses][:current_available].positive?
23:   end
24: 
25:   def get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(limit)
26:     conversations
27:       .open
28:       .where(account_id: account_id)
29:       .select(:assignee_id)
30:       .group(:assignee_id)
31:       .having("count(*) >= #{limit.to_i}")
32:       .filter_map(&:assignee_id)
33:   end
34: 
35:   def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
36:     return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].blank?
37:     return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].to_i.positive?
38: 
39:     errors.add(:auto_assignment_config, 'max_assignment_limit must be greater than 0')
40:   end
41: end

3: module Avatarable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5:   include Rails.application.routes.url_helpers
6: 
7:   ALLOWED_AVATAR_CONTENT_TYPES = %w[image/jpeg image/png image/gif image/webp].freeze
8: 
9:   included do
10:     has_one_attached :avatar
11:     validate :acceptable_avatar, if: -> { avatar.changed? }
12:     after_save :fetch_avatar_from_gravatar
13:   end
14: 
15:   def avatar_url
16:     return url_for(avatar.representation(resize_to_fill: [250, nil])) if avatar.attached? && avatar.representable?
17: 
18:     ''
19:   end
20: 
21:   def fetch_avatar_from_gravatar
22:     return unless saved_changes.key?(:email)
23:     return if email.blank?
24: 
25:     # Incase avatar_url is supplied, we don't want to fetch avatar from gravatar
26:     # So we will wait for it to be processed
27:     Avatar::AvatarFromGravatarJob.set(wait: 30.seconds).perform_later(self, email)
28:   end
29: 
30:   def acceptable_avatar
31:     return unless avatar.attached?
32: 
33:     errors.add(:avatar, 'is too big') if avatar.byte_size > 15.megabytes
34: 
35:     errors.add(:avatar, 'filetype not supported') unless ALLOWED_AVATAR_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(avatar.content_type)
36:   end
37: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module OutOfOffisable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   OFFISABLE_ATTRS = %w[day_of_week closed_all_day open_hour open_minutes close_hour close_minutes open_all_day].freeze
7: 
8:   included do
9:     has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async
10:     after_create :create_default_working_hours
11:   end
12: 
13:   def out_of_office?
14:     working_hours_enabled? && working_hours.today.closed_now?
15:   end
16: 
17:   def working_now?
18:     !out_of_office?
19:   end
20: 
21:   def weekly_schedule
22:     working_hours.sort_by(&:day_of_week).map do |wh|
23:       {
24:         'day_of_week' => wh.day_of_week,
25:         'closed_all_day' => wh.closed_all_day,
26:         'open_hour' => wh.open_hour,
27:         'open_minutes' => wh.open_minutes,
28:         'close_hour' => wh.close_hour,
29:         'close_minutes' => wh.close_minutes,
30:         'open_all_day' => wh.open_all_day
31:       }
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   # accepts an array of hashes similiar to the format of weekly_schedule
36:   #  [
37:   #    { "day_of_week"=>1,
38:   #      "closed_all_day"=>false,
39:   #      "open_hour"=>9,
40:   #      "open_minutes"=>0,
41:   #      "close_hour"=>17,
42:   #      "close_minutes"=>0,
43:   #      "open_all_day=>false" },...]
44:   def update_working_hours(params)
45:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
46:       params.each do |working_hour|
47:         working_hours.find_by(day_of_week: working_hour['day_of_week']).update(working_hour.slice(*OFFISABLE_ATTRS))
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   private
53: 
54:   def create_default_working_hours
55:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 0, closed_all_day: true, open_all_day: false)
56:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 1, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
57:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 2, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
58:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 3, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
59:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 4, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
60:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 5, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
61:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 6, closed_all_day: true, open_all_day: false)
62:   end
63: end

1: class ApplicationRecord < ActiveRecord::Base
2:   MAX_STRING_COLUMN_LENGTH = 255
3:   MAX_TEXT_COLUMN_LENGTH = 20_000
4: 
5:   include Events::Types
6:   self.abstract_class = true
7: 
8:   before_validation :validates_column_content_length
9: 
10:   # the models that exposed in email templates through liquid
11:   def droppables
12:     %w[Account Channel Conversation Inbox User Message]
13:   end
14: 
15:   # ModelDrop class should exist in app/drops
16:   def to_drop
17:     return unless droppables.include?(self.class.name)
18: 
19:     "#{self.class.name}Drop".constantize.new(self)
20:   end
21: 
22:   private
23: 
24:   # Generic validation for all columns of type string and text
25:   # Validates the length of the column to prevent DOS via large payloads
26:   # if a custom length validation is already present, skip the validation
27:   def validates_column_content_length
28:     self.class.columns.each do |column|
29:       check_and_validate_content_length(column) if column_of_type_string_or_text?(column)
30:     end
31:   end
32: 
33:   def column_of_type_string_or_text?(column)
34:     %i[string text].include?(column.type)
35:   end
36: 
37:   def check_and_validate_content_length(column)
38:     length_validator = self.class.validators_on(column.name).find { |v| v.kind == :length }
39:     validate_content_length(column) if length_validator.blank?
40:   end
41: 
42:   def validate_content_length(column)
43:     max_length = column.type == :text ? MAX_TEXT_COLUMN_LENGTH : MAX_STRING_COLUMN_LENGTH
44:     return if self[column.name].nil? || self[column.name].length <= max_length
45: 
46:     errors.add(column.name.to_sym, "is too long (maximum is #{max_length} characters)")
47:   end
48: 
49:   def normalize_empty_string_to_nil(attrs = [])
50:     attrs.each do |attr|
51:       self[attr] = nil if self[attr].blank?
52:     end
53:   end
54: end
55: 
56: ApplicationRecord.prepend_mod_with('ApplicationRecord')

20: class Channel::FacebookPage < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Channelable
22:   include Reauthorizable
23: 
24:   # TODO: Remove guard once encryption keys become mandatory (target 3-4 releases out).
25:   if Chatwoot.encryption_configured?
26:     encrypts :page_access_token
27:     encrypts :user_access_token
28:   end
29:

31:

35:   before_destroy :unsubscribe
36:

38:     'Facebook'
39:   end
40: 
41:   def create_contact_inbox(instagram_id, name)
42:     @contact_inbox = ::ContactInboxWithContactBuilder.new({
43:                                                             source_id: instagram_id,
44:                                                             inbox: inbox,
45:                                                             contact_attributes: { name: name }
46:                                                           }).perform
47:   end
48: 
49:   def subscribe
50:     # ref https://developers.facebook.com/docs/messenger-platform/reference/webhook-events
51:     Facebook::Messenger::Subscriptions.subscribe(
52:       access_token: page_access_token,
53:       subscribed_fields: %w[
54:         messages message_deliveries message_echoes message_reads standby messaging_handovers
55:       ]
56:     )
57:   rescue StandardError => e
58:     Rails.logger.debug { "Rescued: #{e.inspect}" }
59:     true
60:   end
61: 
62:   def unsubscribe
63:     Facebook::Messenger::Subscriptions.unsubscribe(access_token: page_access_token)
64:   rescue StandardError => e
65:     Rails.logger.debug { "Rescued: #{e.inspect}" }
66:     true
67:   end
68: end

1: # == Schema Information
2: #
3: # Table name: channel_whatsapp
4: #
5: #  id                             :bigint           not null, primary key
6: #  message_templates              :jsonb
7: #  message_templates_last_updated :datetime
8: #  phone_number                   :string           not null
9: #  provider                       :string           default("default")
10: #  provider_config                :jsonb
11: #  created_at                     :datetime         not null
12: #  updated_at                     :datetime         not null
13: #  account_id                     :integer          not null
14: #
15: # Indexes
16: #
17: #  index_channel_whatsapp_on_phone_number  (phone_number) UNIQUE
18: #
19: 
20: class Channel::Whatsapp < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Channelable
22:   include Reauthorizable
23: 
24:   self.table_name = 'channel_whatsapp'
25:   EDITABLE_ATTRS = [:phone_number, :provider, { provider_config: {} }].freeze
26: 
27:   # default at the moment is 360dialog lets change later.
28:   PROVIDERS = %w[default whatsapp_cloud].freeze
29:   before_validation :ensure_webhook_verify_token
30: 
31:   validates :provider, inclusion: { in: PROVIDERS }
32:   validates :phone_number, presence: true, uniqueness: true
33:   validate :validate_provider_config
34: 
35:   after_create :sync_templates
36:   before_destroy :teardown_webhooks
37:   after_commit :setup_webhooks, on: :create, if: :should_auto_setup_webhooks?
38: 
39:   def name
40:     'Whatsapp'
41:   end
42: 
43:   # Mirrors Channel::TwilioSms#voice_enabled? so the call subsystem can duck-type across providers.
44:   # Meta's Calling API is available to any whatsapp_cloud inbox (embedded-signup or manual keys);
45:   # only 360dialog (default provider) can't reach the call APIs.
46:   def voice_enabled?
47:     voice_calling_supported? &&
48:       provider_config['calling_enabled'].present? &&
49:       account.feature_enabled?('channel_voice')
50:   end
51: 
52:   # Mutes only the incoming side of calling; default on, so only an explicit false disables inbound.
53:   def inbound_calls_enabled?
54:     provider_config['inbound_calls_enabled'] != false
55:   end
56: 
57:   # Whether this inbox can do WhatsApp calling at all. Meta's Calling API is
58:   # reachable by any whatsapp_cloud inbox, so 360dialog inboxes can't be toggled
59:   # on even though calling_enabled would persist.
60:   def voice_calling_supported?
61:     provider == 'whatsapp_cloud'
62:   end
63: 
64:   def provider_service
65:     if provider == 'whatsapp_cloud'
66:       Whatsapp::Providers::WhatsappCloudService.new(whatsapp_channel: self)
67:     else
68:       Whatsapp::Providers::Whatsapp360DialogService.new(whatsapp_channel: self)
69:     end
70:   end
71: 
72:   # Enables voice: turns calling on at Meta (idempotent), then re-registers webhooks
73:   # with the in-memory calling_enabled flag so the `calls` field is subscribed. The
74:   # flag is persisted only after registration succeeds, so a webhook failure can't
75:   # leave the inbox reporting voice_enabled? while the WABA isn't subscribed to calls.
76:   # Saved with validate: false to skip validate_provider_config's remote credential
77:   # re-check, which could spuriously fail and desync the flag from Meta.
78:   def enable_voice_calling!
79:     raise 'WhatsApp calling requires a whatsapp_cloud inbox' unless voice_calling_supported?
80:     raise 'WhatsApp calling requires the channel_voice feature' unless account.feature_enabled?('channel_voice')
81: 
82:     provider_service.update_calling_status('ENABLED')
83:     self.provider_config = provider_config.merge('calling_enabled' => true)
84:     webhook_setup_service.register_callback
85:     save!(validate: false)
86:   end
87: 
88:   # Disables voice: unsets calling_enabled (gates the call subsystem) and re-registers
89:   # webhooks, which drops `calls` from the subscription (best-effort, so a Meta outage
90:   # can't trap admins). Leaves Meta's WABA calling.status untouched.
91:   def disable_voice_calling!
92:     raise 'WhatsApp calling requires a whatsapp_cloud inbox' unless voice_calling_supported?
93: 
94:     self.provider_config = provider_config.merge('calling_enabled' => false)
95:     save!(validate: false)
96:     begin
97:       webhook_setup_service.register_callback
98:     rescue StandardError => e
99:       Rails.logger.warn "[WHATSAPP CALL] disable webhook re-subscribe failed: #{e.message}"
100:     end
101:   end
102: 
103:   def mark_message_templates_updated
104:     # rubocop:disable Rails/SkipsModelValidations
105:     update_column(:message_templates_last_updated, Time.zone.now)
106:     # rubocop:enable Rails/SkipsModelValidations
107:   end
108: 
109:   delegate :send_message, to: :provider_service
110:   delegate :send_template, to: :provider_service
111:   delegate :sync_templates, to: :provider_service
112:   delegate :media_url, to: :provider_service
113:   delegate :api_headers, to: :provider_service
114: 
115:   def setup_webhooks
116:     perform_webhook_setup
117:   rescue StandardError => e
118:     Rails.logger.error "[WHATSAPP] Webhook setup failed: #{e.message}"
119:     prompt_reauthorization!
120:   end
121: 
122:   private
123: 
124:   def ensure_webhook_verify_token
125:     provider_config['webhook_verify_token'] ||= SecureRandom.hex(16) if provider == 'whatsapp_cloud'
126:   end
127: 
128:   def validate_provider_config
129:     errors.add(:provider_config, 'Invalid Credentials') unless provider_service.validate_provider_config?
130:   end
131: 
132:   def perform_webhook_setup
133:     webhook_setup_service.perform
134:   end
135: 
136:   def webhook_setup_service
137:     Whatsapp::WebhookSetupService.new(self, provider_config['business_account_id'], provider_config['api_key'])
138:   end
139: 
140:   def teardown_webhooks
141:     Whatsapp::WebhookTeardownService.new(self).perform
142:   end
143: 
144:   def should_auto_setup_webhooks?
145:     # Only auto-setup webhooks for whatsapp_cloud provider with manual setup
146:     # Embedded signup calls setup_webhooks explicitly in EmbeddedSignupService
147:     provider == 'whatsapp_cloud' && provider_config['source'] != 'embedded_signup'
148:   end
149: end

(End of file - total 149 lines)
</content>

1: class DeleteObjectJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :low
3: 
4:   BATCH_SIZE = 5_000
5: 
6:   def perform(object, user = nil, ip = nil)
7:     # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid
8:     # timeouts & race conditions due to destroy_async fan-out.
9:     purge_heavy_associations(object)
10:     object.destroy!
11:     process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip)
12:   end
13: 
14:   def process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip); end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def heavy_associations
19:     {
20:       Account => %i[conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events],
21:       Inbox => %i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]
22:     }.freeze
23:   end
24: 
25:   def purge_heavy_associations(object)
26:     klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
27:     return unless klass
28: 
29:     heavy_associations[klass].each do |assoc|
30:       next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)
31: 
32:       batch_destroy(object.public_send(assoc))
33:     end
34:   end
35: 
36:   def batch_destroy(relation)
37:     relation.find_in_batches(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
38:       batch.each(&:destroy!)
39:     end
40:   end
41: end
42: 
43: DeleteObjectJob.prepend_mod_with('DeleteObjectJob')

1: class Api::V1::Accounts::InboxesController < Api::V1::Accounts::BaseController
2:   include Api::V1::InboxesHelper
3:   before_action :fetch_inbox, except: [:index, :create]
4:   before_action :fetch_agent_bot, only: [:set_agent_bot]
5:   before_action :validate_limit, only: [:create]
6:   # we are already handling the authorization in fetch inbox
7:   before_action :check_authorization, except: [:show]
8: 
9:   include Api::V1::Accounts::Concerns::WhatsappHealthManagement
10: 
11:   def index
12:     @inboxes = policy_scope(Current.account.inboxes)
13:                .includes(:channel, :portal, :working_hours, { avatar_attachment: :blob })
14:                .order_by_name
15:   end
16: 
17:   def show; end
18: 
19:   # Deprecated: This API will be removed in 2.7.0
20:   def assignable_agents
21:     @assignable_agents = @inbox.assignable_agents
22:   end
23: 
24:   def campaigns
25:     @campaigns = @inbox.campaigns
26:   end
27: 
28:   def avatar
29:     @inbox.avatar.attachment.destroy! if @inbox.avatar.attached?
30:     head :ok
31:   end
32: 
33:   def create
34:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
35:       channel = create_channel
36:       @inbox = Current.account.inboxes.build(
37:         {
38:           name: inbox_name(channel),
39:           channel: channel
40:         }.merge(
41:           permitted_params.except(:channel)
42:         )
43:       )
44:       @inbox.save!
45:     end
46:   end
47: 
48:   def update
49:     inbox_params = permitted_params.except(:channel, :csat_config)
50:     inbox_params[:csat_config] = format_csat_config(permitted_params[:csat_config]) if permitted_params[:csat_config].present?
51:     @inbox.update!(inbox_params)
52:     update_inbox_working_hours
53:     update_channel if channel_update_required?
54:   end
55: 
56:   def agent_bot
57:     @agent_bot = @inbox.agent_bot
58:   end
59: 
60:   def set_agent_bot
61:     if @agent_bot
62:       agent_bot_inbox = @inbox.agent_bot_inbox || AgentBotInbox.new(inbox: @inbox)
63:       agent_bot_inbox.agent_bot = @agent_bot
64:       agent_bot_inbox.save!
65:     elsif @inbox.agent_bot_inbox.present?
66:       @inbox.agent_bot_inbox.destroy!
67:     end
68:     head :ok
69:   end
70: 
71:   def reset_secret
72:     return head :not_found unless @inbox.api?
73: 
74:     @inbox.channel.reset_secret!
75:   end
76: 
77:   def destroy
78:     ::DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip) if @inbox.present?
79:     render status: :ok, json: { message: I18n.t('messages.inbox_deletetion_response') }
80:   end
81: 
82:   private
83: 
84:   def fetch_inbox
85:     @inbox = Current.account.inboxes.find(params[:id])
86:     authorize @inbox, :show?
87:   end
88: 
89:   def fetch_agent_bot
90:     @agent_bot = AgentBot.accessible_to(Current.account).find(params[:agent_bot]) if params[:agent_bot]
91:   end
92: 
93:   def create_channel
94:     return unless allowed_channel_types.include?(permitted_params[:channel][:type])
95: 
96:     account_channels_method.create!(permitted_params(channel_type_from_params::EDITABLE_ATTRS)[:channel].except(:type))
97:   end
98: 
99:   def allowed_channel_types
100:     %w[web_widget api email line telegram whatsapp sms]
101:   end
102: 
103:   def update_inbox_working_hours
104:     @inbox.update_working_hours(params.permit(working_hours: Inbox::OFFISABLE_ATTRS)[:working_hours]) if params[:working_hours]
105:   end
106: 
107:   def update_channel
108:     channel_attributes = get_channel_attributes(@inbox.channel_type)
109:     return if permitted_params(channel_attributes)[:channel].blank?
110: 
111:     validate_and_update_email_channel(channel_attributes) if @inbox.inbox_type == 'Email'
112: 
113:     reauthorize_and_update_channel(channel_attributes)
114:     update_channel_feature_flags
115:   end
116: 
117:   def channel_update_required?
118:     permitted_params(get_channel_attributes(@inbox.channel_type))[:channel].present?
119:   end
120: 
121:   def validate_and_update_email_channel(channel_attributes)
122:     validate_email_channel(channel_attributes)
123:   rescue StandardError => e
124:     render json: { message: e }, status: :unprocessable_entity and return
125:   end
126: 
127:   def reauthorize_and_update_channel(channel_attributes)
128:     @inbox.channel.reauthorized! if @inbox.channel.respond_to?(:reauthorized!)
129:     @inbox.channel.update!(permitted_params(channel_attributes)[:channel])
130:   end
131: 
132:   def update_channel_feature_flags
133:     return unless @inbox.web_widget?
134:     return unless permitted_params(Channel::WebWidget::EDITABLE_ATTRS)[:channel].key? :selected_feature_flags
135: 
136:     @inbox.channel.selected_feature_flags = permitted_params(Channel::WebWidget::EDITABLE_ATTRS)[:channel][:selected_feature_flags]
137:     @inbox.channel.save!
138:   end
139: 
140:   def format_csat_config(config)
141:     formatted = {
142:       'display_type' => config['display_type'] || 'emoji',
143:       'message' => config['message'] || '',
144:       :survey_rules => {
145:         'operator' => config.dig('survey_rules', 'operator') || 'contains',
146:         'values' => config.dig('survey_rules', 'values') || []
147:       },
148:       'button_text' => config['button_text'] || 'Please rate us',
149:       'language' => config['language'] || 'en'
150:     }
151:     format_template_config(config, formatted)
152:     formatted
153:   end
154: 
155:   def format_template_config(config, formatted)
156:     formatted['template'] = config['template'] if config['template'].present?
157:   end
158: 
159:   def inbox_attributes
160:     [:name, :avatar, :greeting_enabled, :greeting_message, :enable_email_collect, :csat_survey_enabled,
161:      :enable_auto_assignment, :working_hours_enabled, :out_of_office_message, :timezone, :allow_messages_after_resolved,
162:      :lock_to_single_conversation, :portal_id, :sender_name_type, :business_name,
163:      { csat_config: [:display_type, :message, :button_text, :language,
164:                      { survey_rules: [:operator, { values: [] }],
165:                        template: [:name, :template_id, :friendly_name, :content_sid, :approval_sid, :created_at, :language, :status] }] }]
166:   end
167: 
168:   def permitted_params(channel_attributes = [])
169:     # We will remove this line after fixing https://linear.app/chatwoot/issue/CW-1567/null-value-passed-as-null-string-to-backend
170:     params.each { |k, v| params[k] = params[k] == 'null' ? nil : v }
171:     params.permit(*inbox_attributes, channel: [:type, *channel_attributes])
172:   end
173: 
174:   def channel_type_from_params
175:     {
176:       'web_widget' => Channel::WebWidget,
177:       'api' => Channel::Api,
178:       'email' => Channel::Email,
179:       'line' => Channel::Line,
180:       'telegram' => Channel::Telegram,
181:       'whatsapp' => Channel::Whatsapp,
182:       'sms' => Channel::Sms
183:     }[permitted_params[:channel][:type]]
184:   end
185: 
186:   def get_channel_attributes(channel_type)
187:     channel_type.constantize.const_defined?(:EDITABLE_ATTRS) ? channel_type.constantize::EDITABLE_ATTRS.presence : []
188:   end
189: end
190: 
191: Api::V1::Accounts::InboxesController.prepend_mod_with('Api::V1::Accounts::InboxesController')

1: class AccountDeletionService
2:   SOFT_DELETE_EMAIL_DOMAIN = '@chatwoot-deleted.invalid'.freeze
3: 
4:   attr_reader :account, :soft_deleted_users
5: 
6:   def initialize(account:)
7:     @account = account
8:     @soft_deleted_users = []
9:   end
10: 
11:   def perform
12:     Rails.logger.info("Deleting account #{account.id} - #{account.name} that was marked for deletion")
13: 
14:     soft_delete_orphaned_users
15:     send_compliance_notification
16:     DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def send_compliance_notification
22:     AdministratorNotifications::AccountComplianceMailer.with(
23:       account: account,
24:       soft_deleted_users: soft_deleted_users
25:     ).account_deleted(account).deliver_later
26:   end
27: 
28:   def soft_delete_orphaned_users
29:     account.users.each do |user|
30:       # Skip users who are still associated with another account.
31:       next if user.account_users.where.not(account_id: account.id).exists?
32: 
33:       original_email = user.email
34:       user.email = soft_deleted_email_for(user)
35:       user.skip_reconfirmation!
36:       user.save!
37: 
38:       user_info = {
39:         id: user.id.to_s,
40:         original_email: original_email
41:       }
42: 
43:       soft_deleted_users << user_info
44: 
45:       Rails.logger.info("Soft deleted user #{user.id} with email #{original_email}")
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def soft_deleted_email_for(user)
50:     "#{user.id}#{SOFT_DELETE_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
51:   end
52: end

1: class Internal::DeleteAccountsJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :scheduled_jobs
3: 
4:   def perform
5:     delete_expired_accounts
6:   end
7: 
8:   private
9: 
10:   def delete_expired_accounts
11:     accounts_pending_deletion.each do |account|
12:       AccountDeletionService.new(account: account).perform
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   def accounts_pending_deletion
17:     Account.where("custom_attributes->>'marked_for_deletion_at' IS NOT NULL")
18:            .select { |account| deletion_period_expired?(account) }
19:   end

1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
5: 
6:   # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
7:   # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
8:   # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
9:   def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
10:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
11:     token = SecureRandom.uuid
12:     return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
13: 
14:     return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
15: 
16:     # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
17:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
18:     false
19:   rescue StandardError
20:     # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
21:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
22:     raise
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
26:     inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
27:     return unless inbox
28: 
29:     service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
30:     assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
31:     Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
32:   rescue StandardError => e
33:     Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
34:     raise e if Rails.env.test?
35:   ensure
36:     release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
37:   end
38: 
39:   private
40: 
41:   # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
42:   # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
43:   # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
44:   def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
45:     return if token.nil?
46: 
47:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
48:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
49:   end
50: 
51:   def bulk_assignment_limit
52:     ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
53:   end
54: end

16:       end
17:     end
18:   end
19: end

1: require 'net/imap'
2: 
3: class Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob < MutexApplicationJob
4:   queue_as :scheduled_jobs
5: 
6:   def perform(channel, interval = 1)
7:     return unless should_fetch_email?(channel)
8: 
9:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::EMAIL_MESSAGE_MUTEX, inbox_id: channel.inbox.id)
10: 
11:     with_lock(key, 5.minutes) do
12:       process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
13:     end
14:   rescue *ExceptionList::IMAP_EXCEPTIONS => e
15:     Rails.logger.error "Authorization error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
16:   rescue IOError, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, Net::IMAP::NoResponseError, Net::IMAP::BadResponseError, Net::IMAP::InvalidResponseError,
17:          Net::IMAP::ResponseParseError, Net::IMAP::ResponseReadError, Net::IMAP::ResponseTooLargeError => e
18:     Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
19:   rescue LockAcquisitionError
20:     Rails.logger.error "Lock failed for #{channel.inbox.id}"
21:   rescue StandardError => e
22:     ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
23:   end
24: 
25:   private
26: 
27:   def should_fetch_email?(channel)
28:     channel.imap_enabled? && !channel.reauthorization_required?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
32:     inbound_emails = if channel.microsoft?
33:                        Imap::MicrosoftFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
34:                      elsif channel.google?
35:                        Imap::GoogleFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
36:                      else
37:                        Imap::FetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
38:                      end
39: 
40:     inbound_emails.each do |inbound_mail|
41:       process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
42:     end
43:   rescue OAuth2::Error => e
44:     Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
45:     channel.authorization_error!
46:   end
47: 
48:   def should_skip_email?(message_id)
49:     failure_count = Rails.cache.read("email_failures:#{message_id}") || 0
50:     failure_count >= 3
51:   end
52: 
53:   def mark_email_as_failed(message_id)
54:     failure_count = Rails.cache.read("email_failures:#{message_id}") || 0
55:     Rails.cache.write("email_failures:#{message_id}", failure_count + 1, expires_in: 6.hours)
56:   end
57: 
58:   def process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
59:     # Skip if this email has failed multiple times recently
60:     if should_skip_email?(inbound_mail.message_id)
61:       Rails.logger.warn "[IMAP] Skipping problematic email: #{inbound_mail.message_id}"
62:       return
63:     end
64: 
65:     begin
66:       Timeout.timeout(email_processing_timeout) do
67:         Imap::ImapMailbox.new.process(inbound_mail, channel)
68:       end
69:     rescue Timeout::Error
70:       mark_email_as_failed(inbound_mail.message_id)
71:       Rails.logger.error "[IMAP] Email processing timeout (#{email_processing_timeout}s): #{inbound_mail.message_id}"
72:     rescue StandardError => e
73:       mark_email_as_failed(inbound_mail.message_id)
74:       Rails.logger.error "[IMAP] Failed to process email #{inbound_mail.message_id}: #{e.message}"
75:       ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
76:     end
77:   end
78: 
79:   def email_processing_timeout
80:     GlobalConfigService.load('EMAIL_PROCESSING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS', 60).to_i
81:   end
82: end

1: class Inboxes::FetchImapEmailInboxesJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :scheduled_jobs
3:   include BillingHelper
4: 
5:   def perform
6:     email_inboxes = Inbox.where(channel_type: 'Channel::Email')
7:     email_inboxes.find_each(batch_size: 100) do |inbox|
8:       ::Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob.perform_later(inbox.channel) if should_fetch_emails?(inbox)
9:     end
10:   end
11: 
12:   private
13: 
14:   def should_fetch_emails?(inbox)
15:     return false if inbox.account.suspended?
16:     return false unless inbox.channel.imap_enabled
17:     return false if inbox.channel.reauthorization_required?
18: 
19:     return true unless ChatwootApp.chatwoot_cloud?
20:     return false if default_plan?(inbox.account)
21: 
22:     true
23:   end
24: end

1: require 'rails_helper'
2: 
3: RSpec.describe DeleteObjectJob, type: :job do
4:   describe '#perform' do
5:     context 'when object is heavy (Inbox)' do
6:       let!(:account) { create(:account) }
7:       let!(:inbox) { create(:inbox, account: account) }
8: 
9:       before do
10:         create_list(:conversation, 3, account: account, inbox: inbox)
11:         ReportingEvent.create!(account: account, inbox: inbox, name: 'inbox_metric', value: 1.0)
12:       end
13: 
14:       it 'enqueues on the low queue' do
15:         expect { described_class.perform_later(inbox) }
16:           .to have_enqueued_job(described_class).with(inbox).on_queue('low')
17:       end
18: 
19:       it 'pre-deletes heavy associations and then destroys the object' do
20:         conv_ids = inbox.conversations.pluck(:id)
21:         ci_ids = inbox.contact_inboxes.pluck(:id)
22:         contact_ids = inbox.contacts.pluck(:id)
23:         re_ids = inbox.reporting_events.pluck(:id)
24: 
25:         described_class.perform_now(inbox)
26: 
27:         # Reload associations to ensure database state is current
28:         expect(Conversation.where(id: conv_ids).reload).to be_empty
29:         expect(ContactInbox.where(id: ci_ids).reload).to be_empty
30:         expect(ReportingEvent.where(id: re_ids).reload).to be_empty
31:         # Contacts should not be deleted for inbox destroy
32:         expect(Contact.where(id: contact_ids).reload).not_to be_empty
33:         expect { inbox.reload }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
34:       end
35:     end
36: 
37:     context 'when object is heavy (Account)' do
38:       let!(:account) { create(:account) }
39:       let!(:inbox1) { create(:inbox, account: account) }
40:       let!(:inbox2) { create(:inbox, account: account) }
41: 
42:       before do
43:         create_list(:conversation, 2, account: account, inbox: inbox1)
44:         create_list(:conversation, 1, account: account, inbox: inbox2)
45:         ReportingEvent.create!(account: account, name: 'acct_metric', value: 2.5)
46:         ReportingEvent.create!(account: account, inbox: inbox1, name: 'acct_inbox_metric', value: 3.5)
47:       end
48: 
49:       it 'pre-deletes conversations, contacts, inboxes and reporting events and then destroys the account' do
50:         conv_ids = account.conversations.pluck(:id)
51:         contact_ids = account.contacts.pluck(:id)
52:         inbox_ids = account.inboxes.pluck(:id)
53:         re_ids = account.reporting_events.pluck(:id)
54: 
55:         described_class.perform_now(account)
56: 
57:         # Reload associations to ensure database state is current
58:         expect(Conversation.where(id: conv_ids).reload).to be_empty
59:         expect(Contact.where(id: contact_ids).reload).to be_empty
60:         expect(Inbox.where(id: inbox_ids).reload).to be_empty
61:         expect(ReportingEvent.where(id: re_ids).reload).to be_empty
62:         expect { account.reload }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     context 'when object is regular (Label)' do
67:       it 'just destroys the object' do
68:         label = create(:label)
69: 
70:         described_class.perform_now(label)
71: 
72:         expect { label.reload }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
73:       end
74:     end
75:   end
76: end

54: class Conversation < ApplicationRecord

85:     open.where('last_activity_at < ? AND waiting_since IS NULL', Time.now.utc - auto_resolve_after.minutes)

87:   scope :resolvable_all, lambda { |auto_resolve_after|

90:     open.where('last_activity_at < ?', Time.now.utc - auto_resolve_after.minutes)

137:   # Be aware: The precision of created_at and last_activity_at may differ from Ruby's Time precision.
138:   # Our DB column (see schema) stores timestamps with second-level precision (no microseconds), so
139:   # if you assign a Ruby Time with microseconds, the DB will truncate it. This may cause subtle differences
140:   # if you compare or copy these values in Ruby, also in our specs
141:   # So in specs rely on to be_with(1.second) instead of to eq()
142:   # TODO: Migrate to use a timestamp with microsecond precision
143:   def last_activity_at

153:     self.status = open? ? :resolved : :open

224:     handle_resolved_status_change

230:   def handle_resolved_status_change
231:     # When conversation is resolved, clear waiting_since using update_column to avoid callbacks
232:     return unless saved_change_to_status? && status == 'resolved'
233:

252:     return if assignee_id.blank?

254:     self.assignee_agent_bot_id = nil

257:   def determine_conversation_status
258:     self.status = :resolved and return if contact.blocked?
259: 
260:     return handle_campaign_status if campaign.present?
261: 
262:     # TODO: make this an inbox config instead of assuming bot conversations should start as pending
263:     self.status = :pending if inbox.active_bot?
264:   end

267:     # If campaign has no sender (bot-initiated) and inbox has active bot, let bot handle it
268:     self.status = :pending if campaign.sender_id.nil? && inbox.active_bot?
269:   end

276:     return if @unread_count_deletion_data.blank?
277: 
278:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(CONVERSATION_DELETED, Time.zone.now, conversation_data: @unread_count_deletion_data)
279:   end

281:   def notify_conversation_updation
282:     return unless previous_changes.keys.present? && allowed_keys?
283: 
284:     dispatch_conversation_updated_event(previous_changes)
285:   end
286: 
287:   def list_of_keys
288:     %w[team_id assignee_id assignee_agent_bot_id status snoozed_until custom_attributes label_list waiting_since
289:        first_reply_created_at priority]
290:   end
291: 
292:   def allowed_keys?
293:     (
294:       previous_changes.keys.intersect?(list_of_keys) ||
295:       (previous_changes['additional_attributes'].present? && previous_changes['additional_attributes'][1].keys.intersect?(%w[conversation_language]))
296:     )
297:   end
298: 
299:   def load_attributes_created_by_db_triggers
300:     # Display id is set via a trigger in the database
301:     # So we need to specifically fetch it after the record is created
302:     # We can't use reload because it will clear the previous changes, which we need for the dispatcher
303:     obj_from_db = self.class.find(id)
304:     self[:display_id] = obj_from_db[:display_id]
305:     self[:uuid] = obj_from_db[:uuid]
306:   end
307: 
308:   def notify_status_change
309:     {
310:       CONVERSATION_OPENED => -> { saved_change_to_status? && open? },
311:       CONVERSATION_RESOLVED => -> { saved_change_to_status? && resolved? },
312:       CONVERSATION_STATUS_CHANGED => -> { saved_change_to_status? },
313:       CONVERSATION_READ => -> { saved_change_to_contact_last_seen_at? },
314:       CONVERSATION_CONTACT_CHANGED => -> { saved_change_to_contact_id? }
315:     }.each do |event, condition|
316:       condition.call && dispatcher_dispatch(event, status_change)
317:     end
318:   end
319: 
320:   def dispatcher_dispatch(event_name, changed_attributes = nil)
321:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(event_name, Time.zone.now, conversation: self, notifiable_assignee_change: notifiable_assignee_change?,
322:                                                                        changed_attributes: changed_attributes,
323:                                                                        performed_by: Current.executed_by)
324:   end
325: 
326:   def set_unread_count_deletion_data
327:     @unread_count_deletion_data = {
328:       id: id,
329:       account_id: account_id,
330:       inbox_id: inbox_id,
331:       assignee_id: assignee_id,
332:       team_id: team_id,
333:       cached_label_list: cached_label_list
334:     }
335:   end
336: 
337:   def conversation_status_changed_to_open?
338:     return false unless open?
339:     # saved_change_to_status? method only works in case of update
340:     return true if previous_changes.key?(:id) || saved_change_to_status?
341:   end
342: 
343:   def create_label_change(user_name)
344:     return unless user_name
345: 
346:     previous_labels, current_labels = previous_changes[:label_list]
347:     return unless (previous_labels.is_a? Array) && (current_labels.is_a? Array)
348: 
349:     create_label_added(user_name, current_labels - previous_labels)
350:     create_label_removed(user_name, previous_labels - current_labels)
351:   end
352: 
353:   def validate_referer_url
354:     return unless additional_attributes['referer']
355:

23: class ContactInbox < ApplicationRecord
24:   include Pubsubable
25:   include RegexHelper
26:   validates :inbox_id, presence: true
27:   validates :contact_id, presence: true
28:   validates :source_id, presence: true
29:   validate :valid_source_id_format?

31:   belongs_to :contact
32:   belongs_to :inbox
33: 
34:   has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async

37:   scope :stale_without_conversations, lambda { |time_period|
38:     left_joins(:conversations)
39:       .where('contact_inboxes.created_at < ?', time_period)
40:       .where(conversations: { contact_id: nil })
41:   }

44:     {
45:       id: id,
46:       contact: contact.try(:webhook_data),
47:       inbox: inbox.webhook_data,
48:       account: inbox.account.webhook_data,
49:       current_conversation: current_conversation.try(:webhook_data),
50:       source_id: source_id
51:     }
52:   end
53: 
54:   def current_conversation
55:     conversations.last
56:   end

58:   private
59: 
60:   def validate_twilio_source_id
61:     # https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164#regex-matching-for-e164
62:     if inbox.channel.medium == 'sms' && !TWILIO_CHANNEL_SMS_REGEX.match?(source_id)
63:       errors.add(:source_id, "invalid source id for twilio sms inbox. valid Regex #{TWILIO_CHANNEL_SMS_REGEX}")
64:     elsif inbox.channel.medium == 'whatsapp' && !TWILIO_CHANNEL_WHATSAPP_REGEX.match?(source_id)
65:       errors.add(:source_id, "invalid source id for twilio whatsapp inbox. valid Regex #{TWILIO_CHANNEL_WHATSAPP_REGEX}")
66:     end
67:   end
68: 
69:   def validate_whatsapp_source_id
70:     return if WHATSAPP_CHANNEL_REGEX.match?(source_id)
71: 
72:     errors.add(:source_id, "invalid source id for whatsapp inbox. valid Regex #{WHATSAPP_CHANNEL_REGEX}")
73:   end
74: 
75:   def valid_source_id_format?
76:     validate_twilio_source_id if inbox.channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms'
77:     validate_whatsapp_source_id if inbox.channel_type == 'Channel::Whatsapp'
78:   end
79: end

41: class Message < ApplicationRecord

89:     text: 0,

104:   # [:submitted_email, :items, :submitted_values] : Used for bot message types

111:   store :content_attributes, accessors: [:submitted_email, :items, :submitted_values, :email, :in_reply_to, :deleted,

120:   scope :today, -> { where("date_trunc('day', created_at) = ?", Date.current) }
121:   scope :voice_calls, -> { where(content_type: :voice_call) }

141: 
142:   def channel_token

146:   def push_event_data
147:     data = attributes.symbolize_keys.merge(
148:       created_at: created_at.to_i,
149:       message_type: message_type_before_type_cast,
150:       conversation_id: conversation&.display_id,
151:       conversation: conversation.present? ? conversation_push_event_data : nil
152:     )
153:     data[:echo_id] = echo_id if echo_id.present?
154:     data[:attachments] = attachments.map(&:push_event_data) if attachments.present?
155:     merge_sender_attributes(data)
156:   end

158:   def conversation_push_event_data
159:     {
160:       assignee_id: conversation.assignee_id,
161:       unread_count: conversation.unread_incoming_messages.count,
162:       last_activity_at: conversation.last_activity_at.to_i,
163:       contact_inbox: { source_id: conversation.contact_inbox.source_id }
164:     }

167:   def merge_sender_attributes(data)
168:     data[:sender] = sender.push_event_data if sender && !sender.is_a?(AgentBot)
169:     data[:sender] = sender.push_event_data(inbox) if sender.is_a?(AgentBot)
170:     data
171:   end
172: 
173:   def webhook_push_event_data
174:     push_event_data.merge(
175:       content: Messages::WebhookContentNormalizer.normalize(content),
176:       processed_message_content: Messages::WebhookContentNormalizer.normalize(processed_message_content)
177:     )
178:   end
179: 
180:   def webhook_data
181:     data = {
182:       account: account.webhook_data,
183:       additional_attributes: additional_attributes,
184:       content_attributes: content_attributes,
185:       content_type: content_type,
186:       content: webhook_content,
187:       conversation: conversation.webhook_data,
188:       created_at: created_at,
189:       id: id,
190:       inbox: inbox.webhook_data,
191:       message_type: message_type,
192:       private: private,
193:       sender: sender.try(:webhook_data),
194:       source_id: source_id
195:     }
196:     data[:attachments] = attachments.map(&:push_event_data) if attachments.present?
197:     data
198:   end
199: 
200:   # Method to get content with survey URL for outgoing channel delivery
201:   def outgoing_content
202:     MessageContentPresenter.new(self).outgoing_content
203:   end
204: 
205:   # Raw content with survey URL (no markdown rendering) for webhook consumers
206:   def webhook_content
207:     MessageContentPresenter.new(self).webhook_content
208:   end
209: 
210:   def email_notifiable_message?
211:     return false if private?
212:     return false if %w[outgoing template].exclude?(message_type)
213:     return false if template? && %w[input_csat text].exclude?(content_type)
214: 
215:     true
216:   end
217: 
218:   def auto_reply_email?
219:     return false unless incoming_email? || inbox.email?
220: 
221:     content_attributes.dig(:email, :auto_reply) == true
222:   end
223: 
224:   def valid_first_reply?
225:     return false unless human_response? && !private?
226:     return false if conversation.first_reply_created_at.present?
227:     return false if conversation.messages.outgoing
228:                                 .where.not(sender_type: ['AgentBot', 'Captain::Assistant'])
229:                                 .where.not(private: true)
230:                                 .where("(additional_attributes->'campaign_id') is null").count > 1
231: 
232:     true
233:   end
234: 
235:   def save_story_info(story_info)
236:     self.content_attributes = content_attributes.merge(
237:       {
238:         story_id: story_info['id'],
239:         story_sender: inbox.channel.instagram_id,
240:         story_url: story_info['url']
241:       }
242:     )
243:     save!
244:   end
245: 
246:   def send_update_event
247:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(MESSAGE_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, message: self, performed_by: Current.executed_by,
248:                                                                             previous_changes: previous_changes)
249:   end
250: 
251:   def should_index?
252:     return false unless ChatwootApp.advanced_search_allowed?
253:     return false unless incoming? || outgoing?
254:     # For Chatwoot Cloud:
255:     #   - Enable indexing only if the account is paid.
256:     #   - The `advanced_search_indexing` feature flag is used only in the cloud.
257:     #
258:     # For Self-hosted:
259:     #   - Adding an extra feature flag here would cause confusion.
260:     #   - If the user has configured Elasticsearch, enabling `advanced_search`
261:     #     should automatically work without any additional flags.
262:     return false if ChatwootApp.chatwoot_cloud? && !account.feature_enabled?('advanced_search_indexing')
263: 
264:     true
265:   end
266: 
267:   def search_data
268:     Messages::SearchDataPresenter.new(self).search_data
269:   end
270: 
271:   # Returns message content suitable for LLM consumption
272:   # Falls back to audio transcription or attachment placeholder when content is nil
273:   def content_for_llm
274:     return content if content.present?
275: 
276:     audio_transcription = attachments
277:                           .where(file_type: :audio)
278:                           .filter_map { |att| att.meta&.dig('transcribed_text') }
279:                           .join(' ')
280:                           .presence
281:     return "[Voice Message] #{audio_transcription}" if audio_transcription.present?
282: 
283:     '[Attachment]' if attachments.any?
284:   end
285: 
286:   private
287: 
288:   def prevent_message_flooding
289:     # Added this to cover the validation specs in messages
290:     # We can revisit and see if we can remove this later
291:     return if conversation.blank?
292: 
293:     # there are cases where automations can result in message loops, we need to prevent such cases.
294:     if conversation.messages.where('created_at >= ?', 1.minute.ago).count >= Limits.conversation_message_per_minute_limit
295:       Rails.logger.error "Too many message: Account Id - #{account_id} : Conversation id - #{conversation_id}"
296:       errors.add(:base, 'Too many messages')
297:     end
298:   end
299: 
300:   def ensure_processed_message_content
301:     text_content_quoted = content_attributes.dig(:email, :text_content, :quoted)
302:     html_content_quoted = content_attributes.dig(:email, :html_content, :quoted)
303: 
304:     message_content = text_content_quoted || html_content_quoted || content
305:     self.processed_message_content = message_content&.truncate(150_000)
306:   end
307: 
308:   # fetch the in_reply_to message and set the external id
309:   def ensure_in_reply_to
310:     in_reply_to = content_attributes[:in_reply_to]
311:     in_reply_to_external_id = content_attributes[:in_reply_to_external_id]
312: 
313:     Messages::InReplyToMessageBuilder.new(
314:       message: self,
315:       in_reply_to: in_reply_to,
316:       in_reply_to_external_id: in_reply_to_external_id
317:     ).perform
318:   end
319: 
320:   def ensure_content_type
321:     self.content_type ||= Message.content_types[:text]
322:   end
323: 
324:   def execute_after_create_commit_callbacks
325:     # rails issue with order of active record callbacks being executed https://github.com/rails/rails/issues/20911
326:     reopen_conversation
327:     mark_pending_conversation_as_open_for_human_response
328:     set_conversation_activity
329:     dispatch_create_events
330:     send_reply
331:     execute_message_template_hooks
332:     update_contact_activity
333:   end
334: 
335:   def update_contact_activity
336:     sender.update(last_activity_at: DateTime.now) if sender.is_a?(Contact)
337:   end
338: 
339:   def update_waiting_since
340:     clear_waiting_since_on_outgoing_response if conversation.waiting_since.present? && !private
341:     set_waiting_since_on_incoming_message
342:   end
343: 
344:   def clear_waiting_since_on_outgoing_response
345:     if human_response?
346:       Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(
347:         REPLY_CREATED, Time.zone.now, waiting_since: conversation.waiting_since, message: self
348:       )
349:       conversation.update(waiting_since: nil)
350:       return
351:     end
352: 
353:     # Bot responses also clear waiting_since (simpler than checking on next customer message)
354:     conversation.update(waiting_since: nil) if bot_response? && !preserve_waiting_since
355:   end
356: 
357:   def set_waiting_since_on_incoming_message
358:     # Set waiting_since when customer sends a message (if currently blank)
359:     conversation.update(waiting_since: created_at) if incoming? && conversation.waiting_since.blank?
360:   end
361: 
362:   def human_response?
363:     # if the sender is not a user, it's not a human response
364:     # if automation rule id is present, it's not a human response
365:     # if campaign id is present, it's not a human response
366:     # external echo messages are responses sent from the native app (WhatsApp Business, Instagram)
367:     outgoing? &&
368:       content_attributes['automation_rule_id'].blank? &&
369:       additional_attributes['campaign_id'].blank? &&
370:       (sender.is_a?(User) || content_attributes['external_echo'].present?)
371:   end
372: 
373:   def bot_response?
374:     # Check if this is a response from AgentBot or Captain::Assistant
375:     outgoing? && sender_type.in?(['AgentBot', 'Captain::Assistant'])
376:   end
377: 
378:   def dispatch_create_events
379:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(MESSAGE_CREATED, Time.zone.now, message: self, performed_by: Current.executed_by)
380: 
381:     if valid_first_reply?
382:       Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(FIRST_REPLY_CREATED, Time.zone.now, message: self, performed_by: Current.executed_by)
383:       conversation.update(first_reply_created_at: created_at, waiting_since: nil)
384:     else
385:       update_waiting_since
386:     end
387:   end
388: 
389:   def dispatch_update_event
390:     # ref: https://github.com/rails/rails/issues/44500
391:     # we want to skip the update event if the message is not updated
392:     return if previous_changes.blank?
393: 
394:     send_update_event
395:   end
396: 
397:   def send_reply
398:     # FIXME: Giving it few seconds for the attachment to be uploaded to the service
399:     # active storage attaches the file only after commit
400:     attachments.blank? ? ::SendReplyJob.perform_later(id) : ::SendReplyJob.set(wait: 2.seconds).perform_later(id)
401:   end
402: 
403:   def reopen_conversation
404:     return if conversation.muted?
405:     return unless incoming?
406: 
407:     conversation.open! if conversation.snoozed?
408: 
409:     reopen_resolved_conversation if conversation.resolved?
410:   end
411: 
412:   def mark_pending_conversation_as_open_for_human_response
413:     return unless captain_pending_conversation?
414:     return unless human_response?
415:     return if private?
416: 
417:     conversation.open!
418:   end
419: 
420:   def captain_pending_conversation?
421:     false
422:   end
423: 
424:   def reopen_resolved_conversation
425:     # mark resolved bot conversation as pending to be reopened by bot processor service
426:     if conversation.inbox.active_bot?
427:       conversation.pending!
428:     elsif conversation.inbox.api?
429:       Current.executed_by = sender if reopened_by_contact?
430:       conversation.open!
431:     else
432:       conversation.open!
433:     end
434:   end
435: 
436:   def reopened_by_contact?
437:     incoming? && !private? && Current.user.class != sender.class && sender.instance_of?(Contact)
438:   end
439: 
440:   def execute_message_template_hooks
441:     ::MessageTemplates::HookExecutionService.new(message: self).perform
442:   end
443: 
444:   def validate_attachments_limit(_attachment)
445:     errors.add(:attachments, message: 'exceeded maximum allowed') if attachments.size >= NUMBER_OF_PERMITTED_ATTACHMENTS
446:   end
447: 
448:   def set_conversation_activity
449:     # rubocop:disable Rails/SkipsModelValidations
450:     conversation.update_columns(last_activity_at: created_at, updated_at: Time.current)
451:     # rubocop:enable Rails/SkipsModelValidations
452:   end
453: 
454:   def reindex_for_search
455:     reindex(mode: :async)
456:   end
457: end

1: # == Schema Information
2: #
3: # Table name: campaigns
4: #
5: #  id                                 :bigint           not null, primary key
6: #  audience                           :jsonb
7: #  campaign_status                    :integer          default("active"), not null
8: #  campaign_type                      :integer          default("ongoing"), not null
9: #  description                        :text
10: #  enabled                            :boolean          default(TRUE)
11: #  message                            :text             not null
12: #  scheduled_at                       :datetime
13: #  template_params                    :jsonb
14: #  title                              :string           not null
15: #  trigger_only_during_business_hours :boolean          default(FALSE)
16: #  trigger_rules                      :jsonb
17: #  created_at                         :datetime         not null
18: #  updated_at                         :datetime         not null
19: #  account_id                         :bigint           not null
20: #  display_id                         :integer          not null
21: #  inbox_id                           :bigint           not null
22: #  sender_id                          :integer
23: #
24: # Indexes
25: #
26: #  index_campaigns_on_account_id       (account_id)
27: #  index_campaigns_on_campaign_status  (campaign_status)
28: #  index_campaigns_on_campaign_type    (campaign_type)
29: #  index_campaigns_on_inbox_id         (inbox_id)
30: #  index_campaigns_on_scheduled_at     (scheduled_at)
31: #
32: class Campaign < ApplicationRecord
33:   include UrlHelper
34:   validates :account_id, presence: true
35:   validates :inbox_id, presence: true
36:   validates :title, presence: true
37:   validates :message, presence: true
38:   validate :validate_campaign_inbox
39:   validate :validate_url
40:   validate :prevent_completed_campaign_from_update, on: :update
41:   validate :sender_must_belong_to_account
42:   validate :inbox_must_belong_to_account
43: 
44:   belongs_to :account
45:   belongs_to :inbox
46:   belongs_to :sender, class_name: 'User', optional: true
47: 
48:   enum campaign_type: { ongoing: 0, one_off: 1 }
49:   # TODO : enabled attribute is unneccessary . lets move that to the campaign status with additional statuses like draft, disabled etc.
50:   enum campaign_status: { active: 0, completed: 1, processing: 2 }
51: 
52:   has_many :conversations, dependent: :nullify, autosave: true
53: 
54:   before_validation :ensure_correct_campaign_attributes
55:   after_commit :set_display_id, unless: :display_id?
56: 
57:   def trigger!
58:     return unless one_off?
59:     return unless feature_enabled?
60:     return unless mark_processing!
61: 
62:     execute_campaign
63:   end
64: 
65:   private
66: 
67:   def feature_enabled?
68:     inbox.inbox_type != 'Whatsapp' || account.feature_enabled?(:whatsapp_campaign)
69:   end
70: 
71:   def mark_processing!
72:     # Multiple scheduler jobs can pick the same active campaign; lock before flipping status to avoid duplicate sends.
73:     with_lock do
74:       next if completed? || processing?
75: 
76:       processing!
77:     end
78:   end
79: 
80:   def execute_campaign
81:     case inbox.inbox_type
82:     when 'Twilio SMS'
83:       Twilio::OneoffSmsCampaignService.new(campaign: self).perform
84:     when 'Sms'
85:       Sms::OneoffSmsCampaignService.new(campaign: self).perform
86:     when 'Whatsapp'
87:       Whatsapp::OneoffCampaignService.new(campaign: self).perform
88:     end
89:   end
90: 
91:   def set_display_id
92:     reload
93:   end
94: 
95:   def validate_campaign_inbox
96:     return unless inbox
97: 
98:     errors.add :inbox, 'Unsupported Inbox type' unless ['Website', 'Twilio SMS', 'Sms', 'Whatsapp'].include? inbox.inbox_type
99:   end
100: 
101:   # TO-DO we clean up with better validations when campaigns evolve into more inboxes
102:   def ensure_correct_campaign_attributes
103:     return if inbox.blank?
104: 
105:     if ['Twilio SMS', 'Sms', 'Whatsapp'].include?(inbox.inbox_type)
106:       self.campaign_type = 'one_off'
107:       self.scheduled_at ||= Time.now.utc
108:     else
109:       self.campaign_type = 'ongoing'
110:       self.scheduled_at = nil
111:     end
112:   end
113: 
114:   def validate_url
115:     return unless trigger_rules['url']
116: 
117:     use_http_protocol = trigger_rules['url'].starts_with?('http://') || trigger_rules['url'].starts_with?('https://')
118:     errors.add(:url, 'invalid') if inbox.inbox_type == 'Website' && !use_http_protocol
119:   end
120: 
121:   def inbox_must_belong_to_account
122:     return unless inbox
123: 
124:     return if inbox.account_id == account_id
125: 
126:     errors.add(:inbox_id, 'must belong to the same account as the campaign')
127:   end
128: 
129:   def sender_must_belong_to_account
130:     return unless sender
131: 
132:     return if account.users.exists?(id: sender.id)
133: 
134:     errors.add(:sender_id, 'must belong to the same account as the campaign')
135:   end
136: 
137:   def prevent_completed_campaign_from_update
138:     errors.add :status, 'The campaign is already completed' if !campaign_status_changed? && completed?
139:   end
140: 
141:   # creating db triggers
142:   trigger.before(:insert).for_each(:row) do
143:     "NEW.display_id := nextval('camp_dpid_seq_' || NEW.account_id);"
144:   end
145: end

1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentService
2:   pattr_initialize [:inbox!]
3: 
4:   def perform_bulk_assignment(limit: 100)
5:     return 0 unless inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
6:     return 0 unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?
7: 
8:     conversations = unassigned_conversations(limit).to_a
9:     return 0 if conversations.empty?
10:     return 0 if inbox.available_agents.empty?
11: 
12:     assigned_count = 0
13:     conversations.each do |conversation|
14:       assigned_count += 1 if perform_for_conversation(conversation)
15:     end
16:     assigned_count
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def perform_for_conversation(conversation)
22:     return false unless assignable?(conversation)
23: 
24:     agent = find_available_agent(conversation)
25:     return false unless agent
26: 
27:     assign_conversation(conversation, agent)
28:   end
29: 
30:   def assignable?(conversation)
31:     conversation.status == 'open' &&
32:       conversation.assignee_id.nil?
33:   end
34: 
35:   def unassigned_conversations(limit)
36:     scope = inbox.conversations.unassigned.open
37: 
38:     # Apply conversation priority using assignment policy if available
39:     policy = inbox.assignment_policy
40:     scope = if policy&.longest_waiting?
41:               scope.reorder(last_activity_at: :asc, created_at: :asc)
42:             else
43:               scope.reorder(created_at: :asc)
44:             end
45: 
46:     scope.limit(limit)
47:   end
48: 
49:   def find_available_agent(conversation = nil)
50:     agents = filter_agents_by_team(inbox.available_agents, conversation)
51:     return nil if agents.nil?
52: 
53:     agents = filter_agents_by_rate_limit(agents)
54:     return nil if agents.empty?
55: 
56:     round_robin_selector.select_agent(agents)
57:   end
58: 
59:   def filter_agents_by_team(agents, conversation)
60:     return agents if conversation&.team_id.blank?
61: 
62:     team = conversation.team
63:     return nil if team.blank? || team.allow_auto_assign.blank?
64: 
65:     team_member_ids = team.members.ids
66:     agents.where(user_id: team_member_ids)
67:   end
68: 
69:   def filter_agents_by_rate_limit(agents)
70:     agents.select do |agent_member|
71:       rate_limiter = build_rate_limiter(agent_member.user)
72:       rate_limiter.within_limit?
73:     end
74:   end
75: 
76:   def assign_conversation(conversation, agent)
77:     return false unless claim_and_assign(conversation, agent)
78: 
79:     conversation.reload
80: 
81:     rate_limiter = build_rate_limiter(agent)
82:     rate_limiter.track_assignment(conversation)
83: 
84:     dispatch_assignment_event(conversation, agent)
85:     true
86:   end
87: 
88:   # Atomically claim the row so two bulk runs that overlap (the in-flight gate
89:   # is best-effort and can lapse on TTL) can't both assign the same conversation.
90:   def claim_and_assign(conversation, agent)
91:     Current.executed_by = inbox.assignment_policy || inbox
92: 
93:     Conversation.transaction do
94:       locked = inbox.conversations
95:                     .where(id: conversation.id, assignee_id: nil)
96:                     .lock('FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED')
97:                     .first
98:       next false unless locked
99: 
100:       locked.update!(assignee: agent)
101:       true
102:     end
103:   ensure
104:     Current.executed_by = nil
105:   end
106: 
107:   def dispatch_assignment_event(conversation, agent)
108:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(
109:       Events::Types::ASSIGNEE_CHANGED,
110:       Time.zone.now,
111:       conversation: conversation,
112:       user: agent
113:     )
114:   end
115: 
116:   def build_rate_limiter(agent)
117:     AutoAssignment::RateLimiter.new(inbox: inbox, agent: agent)
118:   end
119: 
120:   def round_robin_selector
121:     @round_robin_selector ||= AutoAssignment::RoundRobinSelector.new(inbox: inbox)
122:   end
123: end
124: 
125: AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.prepend_mod_with('AutoAssignment::AssignmentService')

1: class AutoAssignment::AgentAssignmentService
2:   # Allowed agent ids: array
3:   # This is the list of agents from which an agent can be assigned to this conversation
4:   # examples: Agents with assignment capacity, Agents who are members of a team etc
5:   pattr_initialize [:conversation!, :allowed_agent_ids!]
6: 
7:   def find_assignee
8:     round_robin_manage_service.available_agent(allowed_agent_ids: allowed_online_agent_ids)
9:   end
10: 
11:   def perform
12:     new_assignee = find_assignee
13:     conversation.update(assignee: new_assignee) if new_assignee
14:   end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def online_agent_ids
19:     online_agents = OnlineStatusTracker.get_available_users(conversation.account_id)
20:     online_agents.select { |_key, value| value.eql?('online') }.keys if online_agents.present?
21:   end
22: 
23:   def allowed_online_agent_ids
24:     # We want to perform roundrobin only over online agents
25:     # Hence taking an intersection of online agents and allowed member ids
26: 
27:     # the online user ids are string, since its from redis, allowed member ids are integer, since its from active record
28:     @allowed_online_agent_ids ||= online_agent_ids & allowed_agent_ids&.map(&:to_s)
29:   end
30: 
31:   def round_robin_manage_service
32:     @round_robin_manage_service ||= AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService.new(inbox: conversation.inbox)
33:   end
34: 
35:   def round_robin_key
36:     format(::Redis::Alfred::ROUND_ROBIN_AGENTS, inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id)
37:   end
38: end

1: class AutoAssignment::RateLimiter
2:   pattr_initialize [:inbox!, :agent!]
3: 
4:   def within_limit?
5:     current_count < limit
6:   end
7: 
8:   def track_assignment(conversation)
9:     assignment_key = build_assignment_key(conversation.id)
10:     Redis::Alfred.set(assignment_key, conversation.id.to_s, ex: window)
11:   end
12: 
13:   def current_count
14:     pattern = assignment_key_pattern
15:     Redis::Alfred.keys_count(pattern)
16:   end
17: 
18:   private
19: 
20:   def limit
21:     config&.fair_distribution_limit.present? ? config.fair_distribution_limit.to_i : 5
22:   end
23: 
24:   def window
25:     config&.fair_distribution_window&.to_i || 5.minutes.to_i
26:   end
27: 
28:   def config
29:     @config ||= inbox.assignment_policy
30:   end
31: 
32:   def assignment_key_pattern
33:     format(Redis::RedisKeys::ASSIGNMENT_KEY_PATTERN, inbox_id: inbox.id, agent_id: agent.id)
34:   end
35: 
36:   def build_assignment_key(conversation_id)
37:     format(Redis::RedisKeys::ASSIGNMENT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox.id, agent_id: agent.id, conversation_id: conversation_id)
38:   end
39: end

73:     urls = ENV.fetch('VOICE_CALL_STUN_URLS', DEFAULT_STUN_URL).split(',').filter_map { |u| u.strip.presence }
74:     [{ urls: urls }]
75:   end
76: 
77:   def direction_label
78:     DISPLAY_DIRECTION[direction]
79:   end
80: 
81:   def ringing?
82:     status == 'ringing'
83:   end

86:     status == 'in_progress'
87:   end

90:     TERMINAL_STATUSES.include?(status)

103:   end

107: 
108:     Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.rails_blob_url(recording)

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/chatwoot/config/initializers/01_inject_enterprise_edition_module.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # original Authors: Gitlab
4: # https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/blob/master/config/initializers/0_inject_enterprise_edition_module.rb
5: #
6: 
7: ### Ref: https://medium.com/@leo_hetsch/ruby-modules-include-vs-prepend-vs-extend-f09837a5b073
8: # Ancestors chain : it holds a list of constant names which are its ancestors
9: #  example, by calling ancestors on the String class,
10: #  String.ancestors => [String, Comparable, Object, PP::ObjectMixin, Kernel, BasicObject]
11: #
12: # Include: Ruby will insert the module into the ancestors chain of the class, just after its superclass
13: # ancestor chain : [OriginalClass, IncludedModule, ...]
14: #
15: # Extend: class will actually import the module methods as class methods
16: #
17: # Prepend: Ruby will look into the module methods before looking into the class.
18: # ancestor chain : [PrependedModule, OriginalClass, ...]
19: ########
20: 
21: require 'active_support/inflector'
22: 
23: module InjectEnterpriseEditionModule
24:   def prepend_mod_with(constant_name, namespace: Object, with_descendants: false)
25:     each_extension_for(constant_name, namespace) do |constant|
26:       prepend_module(constant, with_descendants)
27:     end
28:   end
29: 
30:   def extend_mod_with(constant_name, namespace: Object)
31:     # rubocop:disable Performance/MethodObjectAsBlock
32:     each_extension_for(
33:       constant_name,
34:       namespace,
35:       &method(:extend)
36:     )
37:     # rubocop:enable Performance/MethodObjectAsBlock
38:   end
39: 
40:   def include_mod_with(constant_name, namespace: Object)
41:     # rubocop:disable Performance/MethodObjectAsBlock
42:     each_extension_for(
43:       constant_name,
44:       namespace,
45:       &method(:include)
46:     )
47:     # rubocop:enable Performance/MethodObjectAsBlock
48:   end
49: 
50:   def prepend_mod(with_descendants: false)
51:     prepend_mod_with(name, with_descendants: with_descendants)
52:   end
53: 
54:   def extend_mod
55:     extend_mod_with(name)
56:   end
57: 
58:   def include_mod
59:     include_mod_with(name)
60:   end
61: 
62:   private
63: 
64:   def prepend_module(mod, with_descendants)
65:     prepend(mod)
66: 
67:     descendants.each { |descendant| descendant.prepend(mod) } if with_descendants
68:   end
69: 
70:   def each_extension_for(constant_name, namespace)
71:     ChatwootApp.extensions.each do |extension_name|
72:       extension_namespace =
73:         const_get_maybe_false(namespace, extension_name.camelize)
74: 
75:       extension_module =
76:         const_get_maybe_false(extension_namespace, constant_name)
77: 
78:       yield(extension_module) if extension_module
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   def const_get_maybe_false(mod, name)
83:     mod&.const_defined?(name, false) && mod&.const_get(name, false)
84:   end
85: end
86: 
87: Module.prepend(InjectEnterpriseEditionModule)

(End of file - total 87 lines)
</content>

1: class Captain::InboxPendingConversationsResolutionJob < ApplicationJob
2:   CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_RESOLVE_ACTIVITY_REASON = 'no outstanding questions'.freeze
3:   CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_HANDOFF_ACTIVITY_REASON = 'pending clarification from customer'.freeze
4: 
5:   queue_as :low
6: 
7:   def perform(inbox)
8:     return if inbox.account.captain_auto_resolve_disabled?
9: 
10:     if evaluate_conversation_completion?(inbox.account)
11:       perform_with_evaluation(inbox)
12:     else
13:       perform_time_based(inbox)
14:     end
15:   ensure
16:     Current.reset
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def evaluate_conversation_completion?(account)
22:     account.feature_enabled?('captain_tasks') && account.captain_auto_resolve_evaluated?
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform_time_based(inbox)
26:     Current.executed_by = inbox.captain_assistant
27: 
28:     resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox).each do |conversation|
29:       create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
30:       conversation.resolved!
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   def perform_with_evaluation(inbox)
35:     Current.executed_by = inbox.captain_assistant
36: 
37:     resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox).each do |conversation|
38:       evaluation = evaluate_conversation(conversation, inbox)
39:       next unless still_resolvable_after_evaluation?(conversation)
40: 
41:       if evaluation[:complete]
42:         resolve_conversation(conversation, inbox, evaluation[:reason])
43:       else
44:         handoff_conversation(conversation, inbox, evaluation[:reason])
45:       end
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def evaluate_conversation(conversation, inbox)
50:     Captain::ConversationCompletionService.new(
51:       account: inbox.account,
52:       conversation_display_id: conversation.display_id
53:     ).perform
54:   end
55: 
56:   def resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox)
57:     inbox.conversations.pending
58:          .where('last_activity_at < ?', auto_resolve_cutoff_time)
59:          .limit(Limits::BULK_ACTIONS_LIMIT)
60:   end
61: 
62:   def still_resolvable_after_evaluation?(conversation)
63:     conversation.reload
64:     conversation.pending? && conversation.last_activity_at < auto_resolve_cutoff_time
65:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
66:     false
67:   end
68: 
69:   def auto_resolve_cutoff_time
70:     Time.now.utc - 1.hour
71:   end
72: 
73:   def resolve_conversation(conversation, inbox, reason)
74:     create_private_note(conversation, inbox, "Auto-resolved: #{reason}")
75:     create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
76:     conversation.with_captain_activity_context(
77:       reason: CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_RESOLVE_ACTIVITY_REASON,
78:       reason_type: :inference
79:     ) { conversation.resolved! }
80:     conversation.dispatch_captain_inference_resolved_event
81:   end
82: 
83:   def handoff_conversation(conversation, inbox, reason)
84:     create_private_note(conversation, inbox, "Auto-handoff: #{reason}")
85:     create_handoff_message(conversation, inbox)
86:     conversation.with_captain_activity_context(
87:       reason: CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_HANDOFF_ACTIVITY_REASON,
88:       reason_type: :inference
89:     ) { conversation.bot_handoff! }
90:     conversation.dispatch_captain_inference_handoff_event
91:     send_out_of_office_message_if_applicable(conversation.reload)
92:   end
93: 
94:   def send_out_of_office_message_if_applicable(conversation)
95:     # Campaign conversations should never receive OOO templates — the campaign itself
96:     # serves as the initial outreach, and OOO would be confusing in that context.
97:     return if conversation.campaign.present?
98: 
99:     ::MessageTemplates::Template::OutOfOffice.perform_if_applicable(conversation)
100:   end
101: 
102:   def create_private_note(conversation, inbox, content)
103:     conversation.messages.create!(
104:       message_type: :outgoing,
105:       private: true,
106:       sender: inbox.captain_assistant,
107:       account_id: conversation.account_id,
108:       inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
109:       content: content
110:     )
111:   end
112: 
113:   def create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
114:     I18n.with_locale(inbox.account.locale) do
115:       resolution_message = inbox.captain_assistant.config['resolution_message']
116:       conversation.messages.create!(
117:         message_type: :outgoing,
118:         account_id: conversation.account_id,
119:         inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
120:         content: resolution_message.presence || I18n.t('conversations.activity.auto_resolution_message'),
121:         sender: inbox.captain_assistant
122:       )
123:     end
124:   end
125: 
126:   def create_handoff_message(conversation, inbox)
127:     handoff_message = inbox.captain_assistant.config['handoff_message']
128:     return if handoff_message.blank?
129: 
130:     conversation.messages.create!(
131:       message_type: :outgoing,
132:       sender: inbox.captain_assistant,
133:       account_id: conversation.account_id,
134:       inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
135:       content: handoff_message,
136:       preserve_waiting_since: true
137:     )
138:   end
139: end
