<type>file</type>
<content>
1: class Voice::InboundCallBuilder
2:   attr_reader :inbox, :from_number, :call_sid, :provider, :extra_meta
3: 
4:   def self.perform!(inbox:, from_number:, call_sid:, provider: :twilio, extra_meta: {})
5:     new(inbox: inbox, from_number: from_number, call_sid: call_sid,
6:         provider: provider, extra_meta: extra_meta).perform!
7:   end
8: 
9:   def initialize(inbox:, from_number:, call_sid:, provider: :twilio, extra_meta: {})
10:     @inbox = inbox
11:     @from_number = from_number
12:     @call_sid = call_sid
13:     @provider = provider.to_sym
14:     @extra_meta = extra_meta || {}
15:   end
16: 
17:   def perform!
18:     existing = find_existing_call
19:     return existing if existing
20: 
21:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
22:       contact_inbox = ensure_contact_inbox!
23:       contact = contact_inbox.contact
24:       conversation = resolve_conversation!(contact, contact_inbox)
25:       call = create_call!(contact, conversation)
26:       message = Voice::CallMessageBuilder.new(call).perform!
27:       call.update!(message_id: message.id)
28:       call
29:     end
30:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
31:     # A concurrent provider retry won the create race; return what now exists.
32:     find_existing_call || raise
33:   end
34: 
35:   private
36: 
37:   def account
38:     inbox.account
39:   end
40: 
41:   def find_existing_call
42:     Call.where(account_id: account.id, inbox_id: inbox.id)
43:         .find_by(provider: provider, provider_call_id: call_sid)
44:   end
45: 
46:   # Always look up by (inbox, source_id) first — that pair has a UNIQUE index, so
47:   # creating with a colliding source_id under a different contact would raise
48:   # RecordNotUnique. Reuse the existing ContactInbox (and its contact) when found.
49:   # A concurrent message webhook for the same wa_id can win the (inbox_id, source_id)
50:   # race; rescue and re-find so the call path doesn't drop the connect.
51:   def ensure_contact_inbox!
52:     sid = source_id_for_provider
53:     existing = inbox.contact_inboxes.find_by(source_id: sid)
54:     return existing if existing
55: 
56:     ContactInbox.create!(contact: ensure_contact!, inbox: inbox, source_id: sid)
57:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
58:     inbox.contact_inboxes.find_by!(source_id: sid)
59:   end
60: 
61:   def ensure_contact!
62:     contact = account.contacts.find_or_create_by!(phone_number: from_number) do |record|
63:       record.name = contact_name.presence || from_number
64:     end
65:     contact.update!(name: contact_name) if contact_name.present? && contact.name == from_number
66:     contact
67:   end
68: 
69:   # WhatsApp inbound calls carry the caller's profile name in extra_meta; Twilio
70:   # calls don't, so contact naming falls back to the phone number.
71:   def contact_name
72:     extra_meta['contact_name'].presence
73:   end
74: 
75:   # WhatsApp ContactInbox.source_id must be digits-only (the wa_id); Twilio accepts the +.
76:   # Run BR/AR-style wa_id normalization (same path messaging uses) so an inbound call
77:   # finds the existing ContactInbox instead of forking a new contact/conversation.
78:   def source_id_for_provider
79:     return from_number unless provider == :whatsapp
80: 
81:     digits = from_number.to_s.delete_prefix('+')
82:     Whatsapp::PhoneNumberNormalizationService.new(inbox).normalize_and_find_contact_by_provider(digits, :cloud)
83:   end
84: 
85:   # Mirror incoming-message routing: reuse the open conversation (or the last one when locked), else create new.
86:   def resolve_conversation!(contact, contact_inbox)
87:     reusable = if inbox.lock_to_single_conversation
88:                  contact_inbox.conversations.last
89:                else
90:                  contact_inbox.conversations.where.not(status: :resolved).last
91:                end
92:     return reusable if reusable
93: 
94:     account.conversations.create!(
95:       contact_inbox_id: contact_inbox.id,
96:       inbox_id: inbox.id,
97:       contact_id: contact.id,
98:       status: :open
99:     )
100:   end
101: 
102:   def create_call!(contact, conversation)
103:     call = Call.create!(
104:       account: account,
105:       inbox: inbox,
106:       conversation: conversation,
107:       contact: contact,
108:       provider: provider,
109:       direction: :incoming,
110:       status: 'ringing',
111:       provider_call_id: call_sid,
112:       meta: { 'initiated_at' => Time.zone.now.to_i }.merge(extra_meta.stringify_keys)
113:     )
114:     # `conference_sid` is a Twilio bridging concept; WhatsApp goes browser↔Meta.
115:     call.update!(conference_sid: call.default_conference_sid) if call.twilio?
116:     call
117:   end
118: end

(End of file - total 118 lines)

<content>
1: class Voice::OutboundCallBuilder
2:   attr_reader :account, :inbox, :user, :contact
3: 
4:   def self.perform!(account:, inbox:, user:, contact:, conversation: nil)
5:     new(account: account, inbox: inbox, user: user, contact: contact, conversation: conversation).perform!
6:   end
7: 
8:   def initialize(account:, inbox:, user:, contact:, conversation: nil)
9:     @account = account
10:     @inbox = inbox
11:     @user = user
12:     @contact = contact
13:     @existing_conversation = conversation
14:   end
15: 
16:   def perform!
17:     raise ArgumentError, 'Contact phone number required' if contact.phone_number.blank?
18:     raise ArgumentError, 'Agent required' if user.blank?
19: 
20:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
21:       contact_inbox = ensure_contact_inbox!
22:       conversation = @existing_conversation || create_conversation!(contact_inbox)
23:       call_sid = initiate_call!
24:       call = create_call!(conversation, call_sid)
25:       message = Voice::CallMessageBuilder.new(call).perform!
26:       call.update!(message_id: message.id)
27:       call

1: class Whatsapp::IncomingCallService
2:   pattr_initialize [:inbox!, :params!]
3: 
4:   def perform
5:     return unless inbox.channel.voice_enabled?
6: 
7:     Array(params[:calls]).each { |c| handle_event(c.with_indifferent_access) }
8:     Array(params[:statuses]).each { |s| handle_status(s.with_indifferent_access) }
9:   end
10: 
11:   private
12: 
13:   def handle_event(payload)
14:     case payload[:event]
15:     when 'connect' then handle_connect(payload)
16:     when 'terminate' then handle_terminate(payload)
17:     else Rails.logger.warn "[WHATSAPP CALL] Unknown call event: #{payload[:event]}"
18:     end
19:   end
20: 
21:   # Meta's `connect` event for outbound calls fires when the WebRTC tunnel is
22:   # up — empirically ~20s before the contact actually answers. The real pickup
23:   # is reported as a separate webhook with status=ACCEPTED, and is what
24:   # `terminate.start_time` aligns to. Treat ACCEPTED as the pickup transition.
25:   def handle_status(payload)
26:     return unless payload[:type] == 'call'
27: 
28:     call = Call.whatsapp.find_by(provider_call_id: payload[:id])
29:     return unless call
30: 
31:     case payload[:status]
32:     when 'ACCEPTED' then mark_outbound_accepted(call, payload)
33:     when 'RINGING' then nil # informational
34:     else Rails.logger.info "[WHATSAPP CALL] Unhandled call status: #{payload[:status]} for #{payload[:id]}"
35:     end
36:   end
37: 
38:   # with_lock + reload here serializes against Whatsapp::CallService (agent
39:   # actions hold call.with_lock across the Meta API call), so a webhook racing
40:   # with a terminate can't overwrite a freshly-finalized terminal status.
41:   def mark_outbound_accepted(call, payload)
42:     call.with_lock do
43:       next unless call.outgoing?
44:       next if call.in_progress? || call.terminal?
45: 
46:       started_at = Time.zone.at(payload[:timestamp].to_i) if payload[:timestamp].present?
47:       update_call!(call, 'in_progress', started_at: started_at || Time.current)
48:       broadcast(call, 'voice_call.outbound_accepted')
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   def handle_connect(payload)
53:     call = Call.whatsapp.find_by(provider_call_id: payload[:id])
54:     if call.nil?
55:       # Only an `offer` payload is a real inbound caller. An `answer` with no
56:       # local row means Meta beat our outbound `Call.create!` (tiny window
57:       # between initiate API response and DB insert) — do not mint an inbound
58:       # row for it; the next status webhook (or a retry) will find it.
59:       return create_inbound_call(payload) if inbound_offer?(payload)
60: 
61:       Rails.logger.warn "[WHATSAPP CALL] Outbound connect for unknown call #{payload[:id]}; skipping"
62:       return
63:     end
64: 
65:     return accept_outbound_call(call, payload) if call.outgoing?
66: 
67:     Rails.logger.info "[WHATSAPP CALL] Duplicate inbound connect for #{payload[:id]}; ignoring"
68:   end
69: 
70:   def inbound_offer?(payload)
71:     payload.dig(:session, :sdp_type).to_s.downcase == 'offer'
72:   end
73: 
74:   def create_inbound_call(payload)
75:     unless inbox.channel.inbound_calls_enabled?
76:       Rails.logger.info "[WHATSAPP CALL] Inbound calls disabled for inbox #{inbox.id}; rejecting call #{payload[:id]}"
77:       inbox.channel.provider_service.reject_call(payload[:id])
78:       return
79:     end
80: 
81:     sdp_offer = payload.dig(:session, :sdp)
82:     extra_meta = { 'sdp_offer' => sdp_offer, 'ice_servers' => Call.default_ice_servers }
83:     name = caller_profile_name(payload)
84:     extra_meta['contact_name'] = name if name.present?
85: 
86:     call = Voice::InboundCallBuilder.perform!(
87:       inbox: inbox, from_number: "+#{payload[:from]}", call_sid: payload[:id],
88:       provider: :whatsapp, extra_meta: extra_meta
89:     )
90:     update_conversation(call)
91:     broadcast_incoming(call, sdp_offer)
92:   end
93: 
94:   # Match strictly on wa_id (== calls[].from): in a batched payload missing this
95:   # call's contact entry, borrowing another caller's name would corrupt this
96:   # contact, so fall back to the phone number (nil here) instead of contacts.first.
97:   def caller_profile_name(payload)
98:     contacts = Array(params[:contacts]).map(&:with_indifferent_access)
99:     match = contacts.find { |c| c[:wa_id].to_s == payload[:from].to_s }
100:     match&.dig(:profile, :name).presence
101:   end
102: 
103:   # `connect` is the WebRTC tunnel-ready signal, not the pickup signal. Apply
104:   # Meta's SDP answer so the handshake completes during ringing; the call
105:   # stays in `ringing` until status=ACCEPTED arrives. Don't gate on
106:   # in_progress: an out-of-order ACCEPTED can flip status before connect is
107:   # processed, and dropping the SDP answer would leave the browser without
108:   # the data it needs to complete the handshake. Use the stored answer as
109:   # the idempotency key instead.
110:   def accept_outbound_call(call, payload)
111:     call.with_lock do
112:       next if call.terminal?
113:       next if call.meta&.dig('sdp_answer').present?
114: 
115:       # Pin setup:active so browsers don't renegotiate when Meta echoes actpass.
116:       sdp_answer = payload.dig(:session, :sdp)&.gsub('a=setup:actpass', 'a=setup:active')
117:       call.update!(meta: (call.meta || {}).merge('sdp_answer' => sdp_answer))
118:       broadcast(call, 'voice_call.outbound_connected', sdp_answer: sdp_answer)
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   def handle_terminate(payload)
123:     call = Call.whatsapp.find_by(provider_call_id: payload[:id])
124:     if call.nil?
125:       # No row yet means either an out-of-order terminate (rare in practice — Meta
126:       # delivery is FIFO) or, more dangerously, an outbound terminate landing in
127:       # the window between the controller's Meta API call and Call.create!.
128:       # Materialising as inbound here would collide with the unique
129:       # (provider, provider_call_id) index. Skip; controller commits seal it.
130:       Rails.logger.warn "[WHATSAPP CALL] Terminate for unknown call #{payload[:id]}; skipping"
131:       return
132:     end
133: 
134:     call.with_lock do
135:       # Webhook retries can re-deliver terminate after we've already finalized the
136:       # call; don't recompute status or a duration=0 retry can flip a completed
137:       # short call back to no_answer.
138:       next if call.terminal?
139: 
140:       duration = payload[:duration]&.to_i
141:       reason = payload[:terminate_reason].to_s
142:       status = derive_terminate_status(call, duration, reason)
143:       meta = (call.meta || {}).merge('ended_at' => Time.zone.now.to_i)
144:       update_call!(call, status, duration_seconds: duration, end_reason: reason, meta: meta)
145:       broadcast(call, 'voice_call.ended', status: call.display_status, duration_seconds: call.duration_seconds)
146:     end
147:   end
148: 
149:   # Provider-reported failures trump the answered/no_answer heuristic. An
150:   # in_progress call that Meta later terminates with a failure reason would
151:   # otherwise be recorded as 'completed' purely because it had been accepted.
152:   FAILURE_REASONS = %w[failed error rejected busy invalid_offer cancelled].freeze
153: 
154:   def derive_terminate_status(call, duration, reason)
155:     return 'failed' if FAILURE_REASONS.any? { |r| reason.include?(r) }
156: 
157:     answered?(call, duration) ? 'completed' : 'no_answer'
158:   end
159: 
160:   # accepted_by_agent_id is the initiating agent on outbound calls, so it only signals "answered" for inbound.
161:   def answered?(call, duration)
162:     call.in_progress? || duration.to_i.positive? || (call.incoming? && call.accepted_by_agent_id.present?)
163:   end
164: 
165:   def update_call!(call, status, **attrs)
166:     call.update!(status: status, **attrs)
167:     Voice::CallMessageBuilder.new(call).update_status!(status: status, agent: call.accepted_by_agent,
168:                                                        duration_seconds: attrs[:duration_seconds])
169:     update_conversation(call)
170:   end
171: 
172:   def update_conversation(call)
173:     call.conversation.update!(
174:       additional_attributes: (call.conversation.additional_attributes || {}).merge(
175:         'call_status' => call.display_status, 'call_direction' => call.direction_label
176:       )
177:     )
178:   end
179: 
180:   # Ring the assignee if any, else online inbox agents, else fall back to the
181:   # inbox's own agents and account admins. Never the whole-account stream, which
182:   # would ring online agents from unrelated inboxes.
183:   def broadcast_incoming(call, sdp_offer)
184:     contact = call.contact
185:     token = call.conversation.assignee&.pubsub_token
186:     streams = token ? [token] : (online_agent_streams.presence || fallback_agent_streams)
187:     broadcast(call, 'voice_call.incoming',
188:               streams: streams,
189:               direction: call.direction_label, inbox_id: call.inbox_id,
190:               sdp_offer: sdp_offer, ice_servers: Call.default_ice_servers,
191:               caller: { name: contact.name, phone: contact.phone_number, avatar: contact.avatar_url })
192:   end
193: 
194:   def online_agent_streams
195:     inbox.available_agents.pluck('users.pubsub_token').compact
196:   end
197: 
198:   def fallback_agent_streams
199:     user_ids = inbox.member_ids | inbox.account.administrators.ids
200:     User.where(id: user_ids).pluck(:pubsub_token).compact
201:   end
202: 
203:   def broadcast(call, event, streams: account_streams, **extra)
204:     payload = { event: event, data: base_payload(call).merge(extra) }
205:     streams.each { |s| ActionCable.server.broadcast(s, payload) }
206:   end
207: 
208:   def account_streams
209:     ["account_#{inbox.account_id}"]
210:   end
211: 
212:   def base_payload(call)
213:     { account_id: inbox.account_id, id: call.id, call_id: call.provider_call_id,
214:       provider: 'whatsapp', conversation_id: call.conversation_id }
215:   end
216: end

5: class ContactInboxWithContactBuilder

8:   def perform

10:   # in case of race conditions where contact is created by another thread
11:   # we will try to find the contact and create a contact inbox

14:   end
15: 
16:   def find_or_create_contact_and_contact_inbox

18:     return @contact_inbox if @contact_inbox

1: # This Builder will create a contact inbox with specified attributes. If the contact inbox already exists, it will be returned.
2: # For Specific Channels like whatsapp, email etc . it smartly generated appropriate the source id when none is provided.
3: 
4: class ContactInboxBuilder
5:   pattr_initialize [:contact, :inbox, :source_id, { hmac_verified: false }]
6: 
7:   def perform
8:     @source_id ||= generate_source_id
9:     create_contact_inbox if source_id.present?
10:   end
11: 
12:   private
13: 
14:   def generate_source_id
15:     case @inbox.channel_type
16:     when 'Channel::TwilioSms'
17:       twilio_source_id
18:     when 'Channel::Whatsapp'
19:       wa_source_id
20:     when 'Channel::Email'
21:       email_source_id
22:     when 'Channel::Sms'
23:       phone_source_id
24:     when 'Channel::Api', 'Channel::WebWidget'
25:       SecureRandom.uuid
26:     else
27:       raise "Unsupported operation for this channel: #{@inbox.channel_type}"
28:     end
29:   end
30: 
31:   def email_source_id
32:     raise ActionController::ParameterMissing, 'contact email' unless @contact.email
33: 
34:     @contact.email
35:   end
36: 
37:   def phone_source_id
38:     raise ActionController::ParameterMissing, 'contact phone number' unless @contact.phone_number
39: 
40:     @contact.phone_number
41:   end
42: 
43:   def wa_source_id
44:     raise ActionController::ParameterMissing, 'contact phone number' unless @contact.phone_number
45: 
46:     # whatsapp doesn't want the + in e164 format
47:     @contact.phone_number.delete('+').to_s
48:   end
49: 
50:   def twilio_source_id
51:     raise ActionController::ParameterMissing, 'contact phone number' unless @contact.phone_number
52: 
53:     case @inbox.channel.medium
54:     when 'sms'
55:       @contact.phone_number
56:     when 'whatsapp'
57:       "whatsapp:#{@contact.phone_number}"
58:     end
59:   end
60: 
61:   def create_contact_inbox
62:     attrs = {
63:       contact_id: @contact.id,
64:       inbox_id: @inbox.id,
65:       source_id: @source_id
66:     }
67: 
68:     ::ContactInbox.where(attrs).first_or_create!(hmac_verified: hmac_verified || false)
69:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
70:     Rails.logger.info("[ContactInboxBuilder] RecordNotUnique #{@source_id} #{@contact.id} #{@inbox.id}")
71:     update_old_contact_inbox
72:     retry
73:   end
74: 
75:   def update_old_contact_inbox
76:     # The race condition occurs when there’s a contact inbox with the
77:     # same source ID but linked to a different contact. This can happen
78:     # if the agent updates the contact’s email or phone number, or
79:     # if the contact is merged with another.
80:     #
81:     # We update the old contact inbox source_id to a random value to
82:     # avoid disrupting the current flow. However, the root cause of
83:     # this issue is a flaw in the contact inbox model design.
84:     # Contact inbox is essentially tracking a session and is not
85:     # needed for non-live chat channels.
86:     raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique unless allowed_channels?
87: 
88:     contact_inbox = ::ContactInbox.find_by(inbox_id: @inbox.id, source_id: @source_id)
89:     return if contact_inbox.blank?
90: 
91:     contact_inbox.update!(source_id: new_source_id)
92:   end
93: 
94:   def new_source_id
95:     if @inbox.whatsapp? || @inbox.sms? || @inbox.twilio?
96:       "whatsapp:#{@source_id}#{rand(100)}"
97:     else
98:       "#{rand(10)}#{@source_id}"
99:     end
100:   end
101: 
102:   def allowed_channels?
103:     @inbox.email? || @inbox.sms? || @inbox.twilio? || @inbox.whatsapp?
104:   end
105: end
106: 
107: ContactInboxBuilder.prepend_mod_with('ContactInboxBuilder')

1: class Internal::RemoveStaleContactInboxesService
2:   def perform
3:     return unless remove_stale_contact_inbox_job_enabled?
4: 
5:     time_period = 90.days.ago
6:     contact_inboxes_to_delete = stale_contact_inboxes(time_period)
7: 
8:     log_stale_contact_inboxes_deletion(contact_inboxes_to_delete, time_period)
9: 
10:     # Since the number of records to delete is very high,
11:     # delete_all would be faster than destroy_all since it operates at database level
12:     # and avoid loading all the records in memory
13:     # Transaction and batching is used to avoid deadlock and memory issues
14:     ContactInbox.transaction do
15:       contact_inboxes_to_delete
16:         .find_in_batches(batch_size: 10_000) do |group|
17:           ContactInbox.where(id: group.map(&:id)).delete_all
18:         end
19:     end
20:   end
21: 
22:   private
23: 
24:   def remove_stale_contact_inbox_job_enabled?
25:     job_status = ENV.fetch('REMOVE_STALE_CONTACT_INBOX_JOB_STATUS', false)
26:     return false unless ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(job_status)
27: 
28:     true
29:   end
30: 
31:   def stale_contact_inboxes(time_period)
32:     ContactInbox.stale_without_conversations(time_period)
33:   end
34: 
35:   def log_stale_contact_inboxes_deletion(contact_inboxes, time_period)
36:     count = contact_inboxes.count
37:     Rails.logger.info "Deleting #{count} stale contact inboxes older than #{time_period}"
38: 
39:     # Log the SQL query without executing it
40:     sql_query = contact_inboxes.to_sql
41:     Rails.logger.info("SQL Query: #{sql_query}")
42:   end
43: end

5:   # https://stackoverflow.com/q/40061982/3824876

8:     prepend InstanceMethods
9:   end

11:   module InstanceMethods
12:     def create_audit_log_entry
13:       account = self.account
14:       associated_type = 'Account'
15: 
16:       return if inbox.nil?
17: 
18:       auditable_id = inbox.id
19:       auditable_type = 'Inbox'
20:       audited_changes = saved_changes.except('updated_at', 'secret')
21: 
22:       return if audited_changes.blank?
23: 
24:       # skip audit log creation if the only change is whatsapp channel template update
25:       return if messaging_template_updates?(audited_changes)
26: 
27:       Enterprise::AuditLog.create(
28:         auditable_id: auditable_id,
29:         auditable_type: auditable_type,
30:         action: 'update',
31:         associated_id: account.id,
32:         associated_type: associated_type,
33:         audited_changes: audited_changes
34:       )
35:     end
36: 
37:     def messaging_template_updates?(changes)
38:       # if there is more than one key, return false
39:       return false unless changes.keys.length == 1
40: 
41:       # if the only key is message_templates_last_updated, return true
42:       changes.key?('message_templates_last_updated')
43:     end
44:   end
45: end

1: module Enterprise::Account
2:   class << self
3:     def captain_document_sync_intervals
4:       parse_captain_document_sync_intervals(InstallationConfig.find_by(name: 'CAPTAIN_DOCUMENT_AUTO_SYNC_INTERVALS')&.value)
5:     end
6: 
7:     private
8: 
9:     def parse_captain_document_sync_intervals(configured_intervals)
10:       return {} if configured_intervals.blank?
11: 
12:       parsed_intervals = configured_intervals.is_a?(String) ? JSON.parse(configured_intervals) : configured_intervals
13:       return {} unless parsed_intervals.is_a?(Hash)
14: 
15:       parsed_intervals.transform_keys { |plan| plan.to_s.downcase }
16:     rescue JSON::ParserError
17:       {}
18:     end
19:   end
20: 
21:   # TODO: Remove this when we upgrade administrate gem to the latest version
22:   # this is a temporary method since current administrate doesn't support virtual attributes
23:   def manually_managed_features; end
24: 
25:   # Auto-sync advanced_assignment with assignment_v2 when features are bulk-updated via admin UI
26:   def selected_feature_flags=(features)
27:     super
28:     sync_assignment_features
29:   end
30: 
31:   def mark_for_deletion(reason = 'manual_deletion')
32:     reason = reason.to_s == 'manual_deletion' ? 'manual_deletion' : 'inactivity'
33: 
34:     result = custom_attributes.merge!(
35:       'marked_for_deletion_at' => 7.days.from_now.iso8601,
36:       'marked_for_deletion_reason' => reason
37:     ) && save
38: 
39:     # Send notification to admin users if the account was successfully marked for deletion
40:     if result
41:       mailer = AdministratorNotifications::AccountNotificationMailer.with(account: self)
42:       if reason == 'manual_deletion'
43:         mailer.account_deletion_user_initiated(self, reason).deliver_later
44:       else
45:         mailer.account_deletion_for_inactivity(self, reason).deliver_later
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     result
50:   end
51: 
52:   def unmark_for_deletion
53:     custom_attributes.delete('marked_for_deletion_at') && custom_attributes.delete('marked_for_deletion_reason') && save
54:   end
55: 
56:   def captain_document_sync_interval(sync_intervals = Enterprise::Account.captain_document_sync_intervals)
57:     plan = custom_attributes['plan_name']
58:     plan = 'enterprise' if plan.blank? && ChatwootApp.self_hosted_enterprise?
59:     return nil if plan.blank?
60: 
61:     interval_hours = sync_intervals[plan.downcase]
62:     return nil unless interval_hours.is_a?(Integer) && interval_hours.positive?
63: 
64:     interval_hours.hours
65:   end
66: 
67:   def saml_enabled?
68:     saml_settings&.saml_enabled? || false
69:   end
70: 
71:   private
72: 
73:   def sync_assignment_features
74:     if feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')
75:       # Enable advanced_assignment for Business/Enterprise plans
76:       send('feature_advanced_assignment=', true) if business_or_enterprise_plan?
77:     else
78:       # Disable advanced_assignment when assignment_v2 is disabled
79:       send('feature_advanced_assignment=', false)
80:     end
81:   end
82:

21:     return if private?

23:

26:     Current.user = nil
27:     Current.executed_by = nil
28: 
29:     begin
30:       conversation.open!

32:

34:     ensure

36:       Current.executed_by = previous_executed_by

6: json.greeting_enabled resource.greeting_enabled
7: json.greeting_message resource.greeting_message
8: json.working_hours_enabled resource.working_hours_enabled
9: json.enable_email_collect resource.enable_email_collect

13: json.auto_assignment_config resource.auto_assignment_config
14: json.out_of_office_message resource.out_of_office_message
15: json.working_hours resource.weekly_schedule
16: json.timezone resource.timezone
17: json.callback_webhook_url resource.callback_webhook_url
18: json.allow_messages_after_resolved resource.allow_messages_after_resolved
19: json.lock_to_single_conversation resource.lock_to_single_conversation
20: json.sender_name_type resource.sender_name_type
21: json.business_name resource.business_name
22: 
23: if resource.portal.present?
24:   json.help_center do
25:     json.name resource.portal.name
26:     json.slug resource.portal.slug
27:   end
28: end
29: 
30: ## Channel specific settings
31: ## TODO : Clean up and move the attributes into channel sub section
32: 
33: json.tweets_enabled resource.channel.try(:tweets_enabled) if resource.twitter?
34: 
35: ## WebWidget Attributes
36: json.allowed_domains resource.channel.try(:allowed_domains)
37: json.widget_color resource.channel.try(:widget_color)
38: json.website_url resource.channel.try(:website_url)
39: json.hmac_mandatory resource.channel.try(:hmac_mandatory)
40: json.welcome_title resource.channel.try(:welcome_title)
41: json.welcome_tagline resource.channel.try(:welcome_tagline)
42: json.web_widget_script resource.channel.try(:web_widget_script)
43: json.website_token resource.channel.try(:website_token)
44: json.selected_feature_flags resource.channel.try(:selected_feature_flags)
45: json.reply_time resource.channel.try(:reply_time)
46: if resource.web_widget?
47:   json.hmac_token resource.channel.try(:hmac_token) if Current.account_user&.administrator?
48:   json.pre_chat_form_enabled resource.channel.try(:pre_chat_form_enabled)
49:   json.pre_chat_form_options resource.channel.try(:pre_chat_form_options)
50:   json.continuity_via_email resource.channel.try(:continuity_via_email)
51: end
52: 
53: ## Facebook Attributes
54: if resource.facebook?
55:   json.page_id resource.channel.try(:page_id)
56:   json.reauthorization_required resource.channel.try(:reauthorization_required?)
57: end
58: 
59: ## Instagram Attributes
60: json.reauthorization_required resource.channel.try(:reauthorization_required?) if resource.instagram?
61: json.instagram_id resource.channel.try(:instagram_id) if resource.instagram?
62: 
63: ## Tiktok Attributes
64: json.reauthorization_required resource.channel.try(:reauthorization_required?) if resource.tiktok?
65: 
66: ## Twilio Attributes
67: json.messaging_service_sid resource.channel.try(:messaging_service_sid)
68: json.phone_number resource.channel.try(:phone_number)
69: json.medium resource.channel.try(:medium) if resource.twilio?
70: if resource.twilio?
71:   json.content_templates resource.channel.try(:content_templates)
72:   if Current.account_user&.administrator?
73:     json.auth_token resource.channel.try(:auth_token)
74:     json.account_sid resource.channel.try(:account_sid)
75:     json.api_key_sid resource.channel.try(:api_key_sid)
76:   end
77: end
78: 
79: if resource.email?
80:   ## Email Channel Attributes
81:   json.email resource.channel.try(:email)
82:   json.forwarding_enabled ENV.fetch('MAILER_INBOUND_EMAIL_DOMAIN', '').present?
83:   json.forward_to_email resource.channel.try(:forward_to_email) if ENV.fetch('MAILER_INBOUND_EMAIL_DOMAIN', '').present?
84: 
85:   ## IMAP
86:   if Current.account_user&.administrator?
87:     json.imap_login resource.channel.try(:imap_login)
88:     json.imap_password resource.channel.try(:imap_password)
89:     json.imap_address resource.channel.try(:imap_address)
90:     json.imap_port resource.channel.try(:imap_port)
91:     json.imap_enabled resource.channel.try(:imap_enabled)
92:     json.imap_enable_ssl resource.channel.try(:imap_enable_ssl)
93:     json.imap_authentication resource.channel.try(:imap_authentication)
94: 
95:     if resource.channel.try(:microsoft?) || resource.channel.try(:google?) || resource.channel.try(:legacy_google?)
96:       json.reauthorization_required resource.channel.try(:provider_config).empty? || resource.channel.try(:reauthorization_required?)
97:     end
98:   end
99: 
100:   ## SMTP
101:   if Current.account_user&.administrator?
102:     json.smtp_login resource.channel.try(:smtp_login)
103:     json.smtp_password resource.channel.try(:smtp_password)
104:     json.smtp_address resource.channel.try(:smtp_address)
105:     json.smtp_port resource.channel.try(:smtp_port)
106:     json.smtp_enabled resource.channel.try(:smtp_enabled)
107:     json.smtp_domain resource.channel.try(:smtp_domain)
108:     json.smtp_enable_ssl_tls resource.channel.try(:smtp_enable_ssl_tls)
109:     json.smtp_enable_starttls_auto resource.channel.try(:smtp_enable_starttls_auto)
110:     json.smtp_openssl_verify_mode resource.channel.try(:smtp_openssl_verify_mode)
111:     json.smtp_authentication resource.channel.try(:smtp_authentication)
112:   end
113: end
114: 
115: ## API Channel Attributes
116: if resource.api?
117:   json.hmac_token resource.channel.try(:hmac_token) if Current.account_user&.administrator?
118:   json.secret resource.channel.try(:secret) if Current.account_user&.administrator?
119:   json.webhook_url resource.channel.try(:webhook_url)
120:   json.inbox_identifier resource.channel.try(:identifier)
121:   json.additional_attributes resource.channel.try(:additional_attributes)
122: end
123: 
124: json.provider resource.channel.try(:provider)
125: 
126: ## Telegram Attributes
127: json.bot_name resource.channel.try(:bot_name) if resource.telegram?
128: 
129: ### WhatsApp Channel
130: if resource.whatsapp?
131:   json.message_templates resource.channel.try(:message_templates)
132:   json.provider_config resource.channel.try(:provider_config) if Current.account_user&.administrator?
133:   # Only show reauthorization for embedded signup; manual flow uses API keys, not OAuth
134:   json.reauthorization_required(
135:     (resource.channel.try(:provider_config) || {}).to_h['source'] == 'embedded_signup' &&
136:     resource.channel.try(:reauthorization_required?)
137:   )
138: end
139: 
140: ## Voice attributes for TwilioSms
141: if resource.twilio? && resource.channel.respond_to?(:voice_enabled?)
142:   json.voice_enabled resource.channel.voice_enabled?
143:   json.inbound_calls_enabled resource.channel.inbound_calls_enabled?
144:   json.voice_configured resource.channel.try(:twiml_app_sid).present?
145:   json.has_api_key_secret resource.channel.try(:api_key_secret).present?
146:   if resource.channel.try(:twiml_app_sid).present?
147:     json.voice_call_webhook_url resource.channel.try(:voice_call_webhook_url)
148:     json.voice_status_webhook_url resource.channel.try(:voice_status_webhook_url)
149:   end
150: end
151: 
152: ## Voice attribute for WhatsApp Cloud (only embedded-signup channels surface true)
153: if resource.channel_type == 'Channel::Whatsapp' && resource.channel.respond_to?(:voice_enabled?)
154:   json.voice_enabled resource.channel.voice_enabled?
155:   json.inbound_calls_enabled resource.channel.inbound_calls_enabled?
156: end

1: # TODO : Delete this and associated spec once 'api/widget/config' end point is merged
2: class WidgetsController < ActionController::Base
3:   include WidgetHelper
4: 
5:   before_action :set_global_config
6:   before_action :set_web_widget
7:   before_action :ensure_account_is_active
8:   before_action :ensure_location_is_supported
9:   before_action :set_token
10:   before_action :set_contact
11:   before_action :build_contact
12:   after_action :allow_iframe_requests
13: 
14:   private
15: 
16:   def set_global_config
17:     @global_config = GlobalConfig.get(
18:       'LOGO_THUMBNAIL',
19:       'BRAND_NAME',
20:       'WIDGET_BRAND_URL',
21:       'DIRECT_UPLOADS_ENABLED',
22:       'MAXIMUM_FILE_UPLOAD_SIZE',
23:       'INSTALLATION_NAME'
24:     )
25:   end
26: 
27:   def set_web_widget
28:     @web_widget = ::Channel::WebWidget.find_by!(website_token: permitted_params[:website_token])
29:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
30:     Rails.logger.error('web widget does not exist')
31:     render json: { error: 'web widget does not exist' }, status: :not_found
32:   end
33: 
34:   def set_token
35:     @token = permitted_params[:cw_conversation]
36:     @auth_token_params = if @token.present?
37:                            ::Widget::TokenService.new(token: @token).decode_token
38:                          else
39:                            {}
40:                          end
41:   end
42: 
43:   def set_contact
44:     return if @auth_token_params[:source_id].nil?
45: 
46:     @contact_inbox = ::ContactInbox.find_by(
47:       inbox_id: @web_widget.inbox.id,
48:       source_id: @auth_token_params[:source_id]
49:     )
50: 
51:     @contact = @contact_inbox&.contact
52:   end
53: 
54:   def build_contact
55:     return if @contact.present?
56: 
57:     @contact_inbox, @token = build_contact_inbox_with_token(@web_widget, additional_attributes)
58:     @contact = @contact_inbox.contact
59:   end
60: 
61:   def ensure_account_is_active
62:     render json: { error: 'Account is suspended' }, status: :unauthorized unless @web_widget.inbox.account.active?
63:   end
64: 
65:   def ensure_location_is_supported; end
66: 
67:   def additional_attributes
68:     if @web_widget.inbox.account.feature_enabled?('ip_lookup')
69:       { created_at_ip: request.remote_ip }
70:     else
71:       {}
72:     end
73:   end
74: 
75:   def permitted_params
76:     params.permit(:website_token, :cw_conversation)
77:   end
78: 
79:   def allow_iframe_requests
80:     if @web_widget.allowed_domains.blank? || embedded_from_non_web_origin?
81:       response.headers.delete('X-Frame-Options')
82:     else
83:       domains = @web_widget.allowed_domains.split(',').map(&:strip).join(' ')
84:       response.headers['Content-Security-Policy'] = "frame-ancestors #{domains}"
85:     end
86:   end
87: 
88:   # Mobile WebViews (iOS/Android) load content from file:// or null origins,
89:   # which cannot match any domain in frame-ancestors. When the per-inbox flag
90:   # is enabled, skip frame-ancestors for these requests.
91:   def embedded_from_non_web_origin?
92:     return false unless @web_widget.allow_mobile_webview?
93: 
94:     origin = request.headers['Origin']
95:     origin.blank? || origin == 'null' || origin&.start_with?('file://')
96:   end
97: end
98: 
99: WidgetsController.prepend_mod_with('WidgetsController')

1: module Api::V1::InboxesHelper
2:   def inbox_name(channel)
3:     return channel.try(:bot_name) if channel.is_a?(Channel::Telegram)
4: 
5:     permitted_params[:name]
6:   end
7: 
8:   def validate_email_channel(attributes)
9:     channel_data = permitted_params(attributes)[:channel]
10: 
11:     validate_imap(channel_data)
12:     validate_smtp(channel_data)
13:   end
14: 
15:   private
16: 
17:   def validate_imap(channel_data)
18:     return unless channel_data.key?('imap_enabled') && channel_data[:imap_enabled]
19: 
20:     # Validate the user-selected auth mechanism before opening the connection.
21:     authentication = Imap::Authentication.validate_user_configurable!(channel_data[:imap_authentication])
22: 
23:     # Use the same auth adapter as the fetch service so LOGIN uses the IMAP LOGIN command,
24:     # not SASL AUTH=LOGIN.
25:     check_imap_connection(channel_data, authentication)
26:   end
27: 
28:   def validate_smtp(channel_data)
29:     return unless channel_data.key?('smtp_enabled') && channel_data[:smtp_enabled]
30: 
31:     smtp = Net::SMTP.new(channel_data[:smtp_address], channel_data[:smtp_port])
32: 
33:     set_smtp_encryption(channel_data, smtp)
34:     check_smtp_connection(channel_data, smtp)
35:   end
36: 
37:   def check_imap_connection(channel_data, authentication)
38:     imap = open_imap_connection(channel_data, authentication)
39:   rescue SocketError => e
40:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.imap.socket_error')
41:   rescue Net::IMAP::NoResponseError => e
42:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.imap.no_response_error')
43:   rescue Errno::EHOSTUNREACH => e
44:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.imap.host_unreachable_error')
45:   rescue Net::OpenTimeout => e
46:     raise StandardError,
47:           I18n.t('errors.inboxes.imap.connection_timed_out_error', address: channel_data[:imap_address], port: channel_data[:imap_port])
48:   rescue Net::IMAP::Error => e
49:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.imap.connection_closed_error')
50:   rescue StandardError => e
51:     raise StandardError, e.message
52:   ensure
53:     imap.disconnect if imap.present? && !imap.disconnected?
54:     Rails.logger.error "[Api::V1::InboxesHelper] check_imap_connection failed with #{e.message}" if e.present?
55:   end
56: 
57:   def open_imap_connection(channel_data, authentication)
58:     imap = build_imap_connection(channel_data)
59:     Imap::Authentication.authenticate!(imap, authentication, channel_data[:imap_login], channel_data[:imap_password])
60:     imap
61:   end
62: 
63:   def build_imap_connection(channel_data)
64:     Net::IMAP.new(channel_data[:imap_address], port: channel_data[:imap_port], ssl: channel_data[:imap_enable_ssl])
65:   end
66: 
67:   def check_smtp_connection(channel_data, smtp)
68:     smtp.open_timeout = 10
69:     smtp.start(channel_data[:smtp_domain], channel_data[:smtp_login], channel_data[:smtp_password],
70:                channel_data[:smtp_authentication]&.to_sym || :login)
71:     smtp.finish
72:   rescue Net::SMTPAuthenticationError
73:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.smtp.authentication_error')
74:   rescue SocketError, Errno::ECONNREFUSED, Errno::EHOSTUNREACH, Errno::ENETUNREACH, Net::OpenTimeout
75:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.smtp.connection_error')
76:   rescue OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError
77:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.smtp.ssl_error')
78:   rescue Net::SMTPServerBusy, Net::SMTPSyntaxError, Net::SMTPFatalError
79:     raise StandardError, I18n.t('errors.inboxes.smtp.smtp_error')
80:   rescue StandardError => e
81:     raise StandardError, e.message
82:   end
83: 
84:   def set_smtp_encryption(channel_data, smtp)
85:     if channel_data[:smtp_enable_ssl_tls]
86:       set_smtp_ssl_method(smtp, :enable_tls, channel_data[:smtp_openssl_verify_mode])
87:     elsif channel_data[:smtp_enable_starttls_auto]
88:       set_smtp_ssl_method(smtp, :enable_starttls_auto, channel_data[:smtp_openssl_verify_mode])
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   def set_smtp_ssl_method(smtp, method, openssl_verify_mode)
93:     return unless smtp.respond_to?(method)
94: 
95:     context = enable_openssl_mode(openssl_verify_mode) if openssl_verify_mode
96:     context ? smtp.send(method, context) : smtp.send(method)
97:   end
98: 
99:   def enable_openssl_mode(smtp_openssl_verify_mode)
100:     openssl_verify_mode = "OpenSSL::SSL::VERIFY_#{smtp_openssl_verify_mode.upcase}".constantize if smtp_openssl_verify_mode.is_a?(String)
101:     context = Net::SMTP.default_ssl_context
102:     context.verify_mode = openssl_verify_mode
103:     context
104:   end
105: 
106:   def account_channels_method
107:     {
108:       'web_widget' => Current.account.web_widgets,
109:       'api' => Current.account.api_channels,
110:       'email' => Current.account.email_channels,
111:       'line' => Current.account.line_channels,
112:       'telegram' => Current.account.telegram_channels,
113:       'whatsapp' => Current.account.whatsapp_channels,
114:       'sms' => Current.account.sms_channels
115:     }[permitted_params[:channel][:type]]
116:   end
117: 
118:   def validate_limit
119:     return unless Current.account.inboxes.count >= Current.account.usage_limits[:inboxes]
120: 
121:     render_payment_required('Account limit exceeded. Upgrade to a higher plan')
122:   end
123: end

1: class Api::V1::Accounts::InboxMembersController < Api::V1::Accounts::BaseController

3:   before_action :current_agents_ids, only: [:create, :update]

5:   def show
6:     authorize @inbox, :show?
7:     fetch_updated_agents
8:   end
9: 
10:   def create
11:     authorize @inbox, :create?
12:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
13:       @inbox.add_members(agents_to_be_added_ids)
14:     end
15:     fetch_updated_agents
16:   end
17: 
18:   def update
19:     authorize @inbox, :update?
20:     update_agents_list
21:     fetch_updated_agents
22:   end
23: 
24:   def destroy
25:     authorize @inbox, :destroy?
26:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
27:       @inbox.remove_members(params[:user_ids])
28:     end
29:     head :ok
30:   end

34:   def fetch_updated_agents
35:     @agents = Current.account.users.where(id: @inbox.members.select(:user_id))
36:   end
37: 
38:   def update_agents_list
39:     # get all the user_ids which the inbox currently has as members.
40:     # get the list of  user_ids from params
41:     # the missing ones are the agents which are to be deleted from the inbox
42:     # the new ones are the agents which are to be added to the inbox
43:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
44:       @inbox.add_members(agents_to_be_added_ids)
45:       @inbox.remove_members(agents_to_be_removed_ids)
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def agents_to_be_added_ids
50:     params[:user_ids] - @current_agents_ids
51:   end
52: 
53:   def agents_to_be_removed_ids
54:     @current_agents_ids - params[:user_ids]
55:   end
56: 
57:   def current_agents_ids
58:     @current_agents_ids = @inbox.members.pluck(:id)
59:   end
60:

62:     @inbox = Current.account.inboxes.find(params[:inbox_id])
63:   end
64: end
